revision.text:
{{神社| 名称=住吉神社| 所在地=兵庫県加東市上鴨川571| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|58|0.25|N|135|3|34.71|E|}}| 祭神=住吉三神、息長足姫命| 創建=不明| 社格=式内小社、村社| 例祭=神事舞（10月第一土日）| 本殿=三間社流造| 画像=[[File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 07.JPG|260px]]
本殿（国の重要文化財）}} '''住吉神社'''（すみよしじんじゃ）は、[[兵庫県]][[加東市]]にある[[神社]]。祭神は[[住吉三神]]（[[上筒之男命]]、[[中筒男命]]、[[底筒男命]]）、[[神功皇后|息長足姫命]]である。[[延喜式神名帳|式内小社]]、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は村社。鎮座地名から'''上鴨川住吉神社'''とも呼ばれる。 == 概要 == 神社の創建は不詳である。 境内は本殿、割拝殿、長床、舞殿が前庭を挟んで配されており、古い神社形式を留めている。 上鴨川村の鎮守社である。本殿は国の[[重要文化財]]に指定されており、[[室町時代]]中期の[[明応]]2年（[[1493年]]）に建造されたことが記録に残っている。 ==文化財== ===重要文化財（国指定）=== *本殿 :三間社[[流造]]、檜皮葺。[[鎌倉時代]]末期の[[正和]]5年（[[1316年]]）に最初の建造がなされ、室町時代中期の[[永享]]6年（[[1434年]]）再建、明応2年（1493年）に再々建され現在に至っている。棟木銘木に建造の記録が残っている。[[江戸時代]]前期の[[貞享]]3年（[[1686年]]）に向拝の修理がなされた。内陣を一段高く造るこの地方独特の形式となっている。床下の転用古材から本来は四間であったことが窺える。[[昭和]]35年（[[1960年]]）6月9日指定。 ===重要無形民俗文化財（国指定）=== *上鴨川住吉神社神事舞 :昭和52年（[[1977年]]）5月17日指定。詳細は''[[上鴨川住吉神社神事舞]]''を参照。 ===兵庫県指定文化財=== *神事能面 :神事舞で使用される[[能面]]で12面が現存する。このうちの、黒色尉面・冠者面・父の尉面・翁面・王鼻面の5面は現在でも神事舞で使用されている。しかし、残り7面（尉面・冠者面・媼面・陵王面）は用途不明である。昭和43年（[[1968年]]）3月29日指定。 == 画像 == File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 02.JPG|参道階段 File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 03.JPG|鳥居と割拝殿 File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 08.JPG|舞殿（右）と長床（左） File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 09.JPG|舞殿 ==参考文献== *兵庫県の歴史散歩編集委員会/編 『兵庫県の歴史散歩 下』 [[山川出版社]] [[2006年]] 61-62ページ *現地説明板 ==外部リンク== {{commonscat|Sumiyoshi-jinja (Kamikamogawa, Kato)}} *[http://club.kobe-np.co.jp/mint/article/odekake/syaji2009111901.html 神戸新聞 社寺巡礼106 上鴨川住吉神社] {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すみよししんしやかとうしかみかもかわ}} [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:加東市]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧村社]] [[Category:式内小社]]

{{神社| 名称=住吉神社| 所在地=兵庫県加東市上鴨川571| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|58|0.25|N|135|3|34.71|E|}}| 祭神=住吉三神、息長足姫命| 創建=不明| 社格=式内小社、村社| 例祭=神事舞（10月第一土日）| 本殿=三間社流造| 画像=[[File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 07.JPG|260px]]
本殿（国の重要文化財）}} '''住吉神社'''（すみよしじんじゃ）は、[[兵庫県]][[加東市]]にある[[神社]]。祭神は[[住吉三神]]（[[上筒之男命]]、[[中筒男命]]、[[底筒男命]]）、[[神功皇后|息長足姫命]]である。[[延喜式神名帳|式内小社]]、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は村社。鎮座地名から'''上鴨川住吉神社'''とも呼ばれる。 == 概要 == 神社の創建は不詳である。 境内は本殿、割拝殿、長床、舞殿が前庭を挟んで配されており、古い神社形式を留めている。 上鴨川村の鎮守社である。本殿は国の[[重要文化財]]に指定されており、[[室町時代]]中期の[[明応]]2年（[[1493年]]）に建造されたことが記録に残っている。 ==文化財== ===重要文化財（国指定）=== *本殿 :三間社[[流造]]、檜皮葺。[[鎌倉時代]]末期の[[正和]]5年（[[1316年]]）に最初の建造がなされ、室町時代中期の[[永享]]6年（[[1434年]]）再建、明応2年（1493年）に再々建され現在に至っている。棟木銘木に建造の記録が残っている。[[江戸時代]]前期の[[貞享]]3年（[[1686年]]）に向拝の修理がなされた。内陣を一段高く造るこの地方独特の形式となっている。床下の転用古材から本来は四間であったことが窺える。[[昭和]]35年（[[1960年]]）6月9日指定。 ===重要無形民俗文化財（国指定）=== *上鴨川住吉神社神事舞 :昭和52年（[[1977年]]）5月17日指定。詳細は''[[上鴨川住吉神社神事舞]]''を参照。 ===兵庫県指定文化財=== *神事能面 :神事舞で使用される[[能面]]で12面が現存する。このうちの、黒色尉面・冠者面・父の尉面・翁面・王鼻面の5面は現在でも神事舞で使用されている。しかし、残り7面（尉面・冠者面・媼面・陵王面）は用途不明である。昭和43年（[[1968年]]）3月29日指定。 == 画像 == File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 02.JPG|参道階段 File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 03.JPG|鳥居と割拝殿 File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 08.JPG|舞殿（右）と長床（左） File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 09.JPG|舞殿 ==参考文献== *兵庫県の歴史散歩編集委員会/編 『兵庫県の歴史散歩 下』 [[山川出版社]] [[2006年]] 61-62ページ *現地説明板 ==外部リンク== {{commonscat|Sumiyoshi-jinja (Kamikamogawa, Kato)}} *[http://club.kobe-np.co.jp/mint/article/odekake/syaji2009111901.html 神戸新聞 社寺巡礼106 上鴨川住吉神社] {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すみよししんしやかとうしかみかもかわ}} [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:兵庫県の重要文化財]] [[Category:加東市]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧村社]] [[Category:式内小社]] [[Category:住吉神社]]

鳥居と割拝殿 |地図=Japan Hyogo }} '''住吉神社'''（すみよしじんじゃ）は、[[兵庫県]][[加東市]]にある[[神社]]。祭神は[[住吉三神]]（上筒之男命、中筒男命、底筒男命）、[[神功皇后|息長足姫命]]である。[[延喜式神名帳|式内小社]]、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は村社。鎮座地名から'''上鴨川住吉神社'''とも呼ばれる。 == 概要 == 神社の創建は不詳である。 境内は本殿、割拝殿、長床、舞殿が前庭を挟んで配されており、古い神社形式を留めている。 上鴨川村の鎮守社である。本殿は国の[[重要文化財]]に指定されており、[[室町時代]]中期の[[明応]]2年（[[1493年]]）に建造されたことが記録に残っている。 ==文化財== [[ファイル:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 07.JPG|thumb|本殿（国の重要文化財）]] ===重要文化財（国指定）=== *本殿 *:三間社[[流造]]、檜皮葺。[[鎌倉時代]]末期の[[正和]]5年（[[1316年]]）に最初の建造がなされ、室町時代中期の[[永享]]6年（[[1434年]]）再建、明応2年（1493年）に再々建され現在に至っている。棟木銘木に建造の記録が残っている。[[江戸時代]]前期の[[貞享]]3年（[[1686年]]）に向拝の修理がなされた。内陣を一段高く造るこの地方独特の形式となっている。床下の転用古材から本来は四間であったことが窺える。[[昭和]]35年（[[1960年]]）6月9日指定。 ===重要無形民俗文化財（国指定）=== *上鴨川住吉神社神事舞 *:昭和52年（[[1977年]]）5月17日指定。詳細は''[[上鴨川住吉神社神事舞]]''を参照。 ===兵庫県指定文化財=== *神事能面 *:神事舞で使用される[[能面]]で12面が現存する。このうちの、黒色尉面・冠者面・父の尉面・翁面・王鼻面の5面は現在でも神事舞で使用されている。しかし、残り7面（尉面・冠者面・媼面・陵王面）は用途不明である。昭和43年（[[1968年]]）3月29日指定。 == 画像 == ファイル:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 02.JPG|参道階段 ファイル:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 08.JPG|舞殿（右）と長床（左） ファイル:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 09.JPG|舞殿 ==参考文献== *兵庫県の歴史散歩編集委員会/編 『兵庫県の歴史散歩 下』 [[山川出版社]] [[2006年]] 61-62ページ *現地説明板 ==関連項目== {{commonscat|Sumiyoshi-jinja (Kamikamogawa, Kato)}} *[[兵庫県指定文化財一覧]] ==外部リンク== *[https://www.kato-kanko.jp/2017/03/sumiyoshi-shrine/ 上鴨川住吉神社]（加東市観光協会） {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すみよししんしや かとうしかみかもかわ}} [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:兵庫県の重要文化財]] [[Category:加東市の建築物]] [[Category:加東市の歴史]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧村社]] [[Category:播磨国の式内社]] [[Category:住吉神社]] [[Category:室町時代の建築]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine |Name=Sumiyoshi Shrine |Location=[[Hyogo Prefecture]], [[Kato City]], Kamikamo River 571 | Latitude degrees = 34 | Latitude minutes = 58 | Latitude seconds = 0.3 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 135 | Longitude minutes = 3 | Longitude seconds = 34.4 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Deities=[[Sumiyoshi Sanjin]]
[[Empress Jingū|Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto]] | Founded=Unknown | Shrine rank=Shikinaisha (small shrine), Village shrine | Annual festival=Shinji Dance (First Saturday and Sunday of October) | Main hall=[[Sanctuary with nagare-zukuri style]] | Image=[[File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 03.JPG|300px]]
Torii and divided worship hall | Map=Japan Hyogo }} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Kato City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]]. The enshrined deities are the [[Sumiyoshi Sanjin]] (Kamutsutsuno-o no Mikoto, Nakatsutsuno-o no Mikoto, Sokotsutsuno-o no Mikoto) and [[Empress Jingū|Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha (small shrine)|ja|式内小社}} under the [[Engishiki Jinmyocho]], and its former shrine rank under the {{ill|Modern shrine rank system|ja|近代社格制度}} was that of a village shrine. It is also referred to as the '''Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine''' due to its location. == Overview == The founding of the shrine is unknown. The shrine precinct features a main hall, a divided worship hall (割拝殿), a long hall (長床), and a dance stage (舞殿), arranged around a central courtyard, preserving an ancient shrine layout. It served as the tutelary shrine of Kamikamogawa Village. The main hall is designated as an [[Important Cultural Property]] of Japan and is recorded to have been constructed in the mid-[[Muromachi period]] during the second year of {{ill|Meiō|ja|明応}} (1493). == Cultural Properties == [[File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 07.JPG|thumb|Main hall (National Important Cultural Property)]] === Important Cultural Property (Nationally Designated) === * Main Hall *: The main hall, constructed in the {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} style with a cypress bark roof, was first built in the late {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}} in the fifth year of {{ill|Shōwa|ja|正和}} (1316). It was rebuilt during the mid-Muromachi period in the sixth year of {{ill|Eikyō|ja|永享}} (1434), and again in 1493 during Meiō 2. Records of its construction are preserved in inscriptions on ridge beams. The porch underwent repairs in the third year of {{ill|Jōkyō|ja|貞享}} (1686). It features a unique regional style, with the inner sanctuary raised on a higher platform. Reused old materials under the floor suggest the structure was originally a four-bay building. Designated on June 9, 1960. === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Nationally Designated) === * Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine Shinji Dance *: Designated on May 17, 1977. For details, see ''[[Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine Shinji Dance]]''. === Hyogo Prefectural Cultural Property === * Shinji Noh Masks *: Twelve noh masks used for the sacred dance survive. Of these, five masks—black old man, young man, father, old man, and king—are still used in ceremonies today. The remaining seven masks (old man, young man, old woman, prince, etc.) have unclear uses. Designated on March 29, 1968. == Gallery == File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 02.JPG|Approach staircase File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 08.JPG|Dance stage (right) and long hall (left) File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 09.JPG|Dance stage == References == * Editorial Committee on Hyogo Prefecture Historical Walks / ed., ''Historical Walks of Hyogo Prefecture, Vol. 2,'' {{ill|Yamakawa Shuppansha|ja|山川出版社}} (2006), pp. 61–62. * On-site explanation board. == Related Articles == {{commonscat|Sumiyoshi-jinja (Kamikamogawa, Kato)}} * {{ill|List of Hyogo Prefectural Cultural Properties|ja|兵庫県指定文化財一覧}} == External Links == * [https://www.kato-kanko.jp/2017/03/sumiyoshi-shrine/ Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine] (Kato City Tourism Association) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Hyogo Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan in Hyogo Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kato, Hyogo]] [[Category:History of Kato, Hyogo]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha shrines in Harima Province]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi Shrines]] [[Category:Muromachi-period architecture]] [[ja:住吉神社 (加東市上鴨川)]]

Designated on March 29, 1968. == Gallery == File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 02.JPG|Approach staircase File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 08.JPG|Dance stage (right) and long hall (left) File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 09.JPG|Dance stage == References == * Editorial Committee on Hyogo Prefecture Historical Walks / ed., ''Historical Walks of Hyogo Prefecture, Vol. 2,'' {{ill|Yamakawa Shuppansha|ja|山川出版社}} (2006), pp. 61–62. * On-site explanation board. == Related Articles == {{commonscat|Sumiyoshi-jinja (Kamikamogawa, Kato)}} * {{ill|List of Hyogo Prefectural Cultural Properties|ja|兵庫県指定文化財一覧}} == External Links == * [https://www.kato-kanko.jp/2017/03/sumiyoshi-shrine/ Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine] (Kato City Tourism Association) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Hyogo Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan in Hyogo Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kato, Hyogo]] [[Category:History of Kato, Hyogo]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha shrines in Harima Province]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi Shrines]] [[Category:Muromachi-period architecture]] [[Category:Kami-Kamikawa Sumiyoshi Shrine]][[Category:Kami-Kamikawa Sumiyoshi Shrine]][[Category:Kami-Kamikawa Sumiyoshi Shrine]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[ja:住吉神社 (加東市上鴨川)]]

{{Shrine |name=Sumiyoshi Shrine |location=[[Hyogo Prefecture]], [[Kato City]], Kamikamo River 571 | Latitude degrees = 34 | Latitude minutes = 58 | Latitude seconds = 0.3 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 135 | Longitude minutes = 3 | Longitude seconds = 34.4 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Deities=[[Sumiyoshi Sanjin]]
[[Empress Jingū|Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto]] | consecration_year=Unknown | Shrine rank=Shikinaisha (small shrine), Village shrine | Annual festival=Shinji Dance (First Saturday and Sunday of October) | Main hall=[[Sanctuary with nagare-zukuri style]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 03.JPG|300px]]
Torii and divided worship hall | Map=Japan Hyogo }} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Kato City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]]. The enshrined deities are the [[Sumiyoshi Sanjin]] (Kamutsutsuno-o no Mikoto, Nakatsutsuno-o no Mikoto, Sokotsutsuno-o no Mikoto) and [[Empress Jingū|Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha (small shrine)|ja|式内小社}} under the [[Engishiki Jinmyocho]], and its former shrine rank under the {{ill|Modern shrine rank system|ja|近代社格制度}} was that of a village shrine. It is also referred to as the '''Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine''' due to its location. == Overview == The founding of the shrine is unknown. The shrine precinct features a main hall, a divided worship hall (割拝殿), a long hall (長床), and a dance stage (舞殿), arranged around a central courtyard, preserving an ancient shrine layout. It served as the tutelary shrine of Kamikamogawa Village. The main hall is designated as an [[Important Cultural Property]] of Japan and is recorded to have been constructed in the mid-[[Muromachi period]] during the second year of {{ill|Meiō|ja|明応}} (1493). == Cultural Properties == [[File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 07.JPG|thumb|Main hall (National Important Cultural Property)]] === Important Cultural Property (Nationally Designated) === * Main Hall *: The main hall, constructed in the {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} style with a cypress bark roof, was first built in the late {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}} in the fifth year of {{ill|Shōwa|ja|正和}} (1316). It was rebuilt during the mid-Muromachi period in the sixth year of {{ill|Eikyō|ja|永享}} (1434), and again in 1493 during Meiō 2. Records of its construction are preserved in inscriptions on ridge beams. The porch underwent repairs in the third year of {{ill|Jōkyō|ja|貞享}} (1686). It features a unique regional style, with the inner sanctuary raised on a higher platform. Reused old materials under the floor suggest the structure was originally a four-bay building. Designated on June 9, 1960. === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Nationally Designated) === * Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine Shinji Dance *: Designated on May 17, 1977. For details, see ''[[Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine Shinji Dance]]''. === Hyogo Prefectural Cultural Property === * Shinji Noh Masks *: Twelve noh masks used for the sacred dance survive. Of these, five masks—black old man, young man, father, old man, and king—are still used in ceremonies today. The remaining seven masks (old man, young man, old woman, prince, etc.) have unclear uses. Designated on March 29, 1968. == Gallery == File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 02.JPG|Approach staircase File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 08.JPG|Dance stage (right) and long hall (left) File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 09.JPG|Dance stage == References == * Editorial Committee on Hyogo Prefecture Historical Walks / ed., ''Historical Walks of Hyogo Prefecture, Vol. 2,'' {{ill|Yamakawa Shuppansha|ja|山川出版社}} (2006), pp. 61–62. * On-site explanation board. == Related Articles == {{commonscat|Sumiyoshi-jinja (Kamikamogawa, Kato)}} * {{ill|List of Hyogo Prefectural Cultural Properties|ja|兵庫県指定文化財一覧}} == External Links == * [https://www.kato-kanko.jp/2017/03/sumiyoshi-shrine/ Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine] (Kato City Tourism Association) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Hyogo Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan in Hyogo Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kato, Hyogo]] [[Category:History of Kato, Hyogo]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha shrines in Harima Province]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi Shrines]] [[Category:Muromachi-period architecture]] [[Category:Kami-Kamikawa Sumiyoshi Shrine]][[Category:Kami-Kamikawa Sumiyoshi Shrine]][[Category:Kami-Kamikawa Sumiyoshi Shrine]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[ja:住吉神社 (加東市上鴨川)]]

The enshrined deities are the [[Sumiyoshi Sanjin]] (Kamutsutsuno-o no Mikoto, Nakatsutsuno-o no Mikoto, Sokotsutsuno-o no Mikoto) and [[Empress Jingū|Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha (small shrine)|ja|式内小社}} under the [[Engishiki Jinmyocho]], and its former shrine rank under the {{ill|Modern shrine rank system|ja|近代type制度}} was that of a village shrine. It is also referred to as the '''Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine''' due to its location. == Overview == The founding of the shrine is unknown. The shrine precinct features a main hall, a divided worship hall (割拝殿), a long hall (長床), and a dance stage (舞殿), arranged around a central courtyard, preserving an ancient shrine layout. It served as the tutelary shrine of Kamikamogawa Village. The main hall is designated as an [[Important Cultural Property]] of Japan and is recorded to have been constructed in the mid-[[Muromachi period]] during the second year of {{ill|Meiō|ja|明応}} (1493). == Cultural Properties == [[File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 07.JPG|thumb|Main hall (National Important Cultural Property)]] === Important Cultural Property (Nationally Designated) === * Main Hall *: The main hall, constructed in the {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} style with a cypress bark roof, was first built in the late {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}} in the fifth year of {{ill|Shōwa|ja|正和}} (1316). It was rebuilt during the mid-Muromachi period in the sixth year of {{ill|Eikyō|ja|永享}} (1434), and again in 1493 during Meiō 2. Records of its construction are preserved in inscriptions on ridge beams. The porch underwent repairs in the third year of {{ill|Jōkyō|ja|貞享}} (1686). It features a unique regional style, with the inner sanctuary raised on a higher platform. Reused old materials under the floor suggest the structure was originally a four-bay building. Designated on June 9, 1960. === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Nationally Designated) === * Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine Shinji Dance *: Designated on May 17, 1977. For details, see ''[[Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine Shinji Dance]]''. === Hyogo Prefectural Cultural Property === * Shinji Noh Masks *: Twelve noh masks used for the sacred dance survive. Of these, five masks—black old man, young man, father, old man, and king—are still used in ceremonies today. The remaining seven masks (old man, young man, old woman, prince, etc.) have unclear uses. Designated on March 29, 1968. == Gallery == File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 02.JPG|Approach staircase File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 08.JPG|Dance stage (right) and long hall (left) File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 09.JPG|Dance stage == References == * Editorial Committee on Hyogo Prefecture Historical Walks / ed., ''Historical Walks of Hyogo Prefecture, Vol. 2,'' {{ill|Yamakawa Shuppansha|ja|山川出版社}} (2006), pp. 61–62. * On-site explanation board. == Related Articles == {{commonscat|Sumiyoshi-jinja (Kamikamogawa, Kato)}} * {{ill|List of Hyogo Prefectural Cultural Properties|ja|兵庫県指定文化財一覧}} == External Links == * [https://www.kato-kanko.jp/2017/03/sumiyoshi-shrine/ Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine] (Kato City Tourism Association) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Hyogo Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan in Hyogo Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kato, Hyogo]] [[Category:History of Kato, Hyogo]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha shrines in Harima Province]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi Shrines]] [[Category:Muromachi-period architecture]] [[Category:Kami-Kamikawa Sumiyoshi Shrine]][[Category:Kami-Kamikawa Sumiyoshi Shrine]][[Category:Kami-Kamikawa Sumiyoshi Shrine]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[ja:住吉神社 (加東市上鴨川)]] [[Category:pages of uncertain originality]] [[Category:pages of uncertain originality]]

It was rebuilt during the mid-Muromachi period in the sixth year of {{ill|Eikyō|ja|永享}} (1434), and again in 1493 during Meiō 2. Records of its construction are preserved in inscriptions on ridge beams. The porch underwent repairs in the third year of {{ill|Jōkyō|ja|貞享}} (1686). It features a unique regional style, with the inner sanctuary raised on a higher platform. Reused old materials under the floor suggest the structure was originally a four-bay building. Designated on June 9, 1960. === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Nationally Designated) === * Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine Shinji Dance *: Designated on May 17, 1977. For details, see ''[[Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine Shinji Dance]]''. === Hyogo Prefectural Cultural Property === * Shinji Noh Masks *: Twelve noh masks used for the sacred dance survive. Of these, five masks—black old man, young man, father, old man, and king—are still used in ceremonies today. The remaining seven masks (old man, young man, old woman, prince, etc.) have unclear uses. Designated on March 29, 1968. == Gallery == File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 02.JPG|Approach staircase File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 08.JPG|Dance stage (right) and long hall (left) File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 09.JPG|Dance stage == References == * Editorial Committee on Hyogo Prefecture Historical Walks / ed., ''Historical Walks of Hyogo Prefecture, Vol. 2,'' {{ill|Yamakawa Shuppansha|ja|山川出版社}} (2006), pp. 61–62. * On-site explanation board. == Related Articles == {{commonscat|Sumiyoshi-jinja (Kamikamogawa, Kato)}} * {{ill|List of Hyogo Prefectural Cultural Properties|ja|兵庫県指定文化財一覧}} == External Links == * [https://www.kato-kanko.jp/2017/03/sumiyoshi-shrine/ Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine] (Kato City Tourism Association) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Hyogo Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan in Hyogo Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kato, Hyogo]] [[Category:History of Kato, Hyogo]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha shrines in Harima Province]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi Shrines]] [[Category:Muromachi-period architecture]] [[Category:Kami-Kamikawa Sumiyoshi Shrine]][[Category:Kami-Kamikawa Sumiyoshi Shrine]][[Category:Kami-Kamikawa Sumiyoshi Shrine]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[ja:住吉神社 (加東市上鴨川)]] [[Category:null category]][[Category:new pages most likely untranslated or similar]] [[Category:null category]][[Category:new pages most likely untranslated or similar]] [[Category:null category]][[Category:new pages most likely untranslated or similar]][[sn:Kami-Kamikawa Sumiyoshi Shrine]][[Category:null category]][[Category:new pages most likely untranslated or similar]] [[Category:qqq]][[sn:Kami-Kamikawa Sumiyoshi Shrine]][[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kami-Kamikawa Sumiyoshi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kami-Kamikawa Sumiyoshi Shrine}}

The enshrined deities are the [[Sumiyoshi Sanjin]] (Kamutsutsuno-o no Mikoto, Nakatsutsuno-o no Mikoto, Sokotsutsuno-o no Mikoto) and [[Empress Jingū|Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha (small shrine)|ja|式内小社}} under the [[Engishiki Jinmyocho]], and its former shrine rank under the {{ill|Modern shrine rank system|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was that of a village shrine. It is also referred to as the '''Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine''' due to its location. == Overview == The founding of the shrine is unknown. The shrine precinct features a main hall, a divided worship hall (割拝殿), a long hall (長床), and a dance stage (舞殿), arranged around a central courtyard, preserving an ancient shrine layout. It served as the tutelary shrine of Kamikamogawa Village. The main hall is designated as an [[Important Cultural Property]] of Japan and is recorded to have been constructed in the mid-[[Muromachi period]] during the second year of {{ill|Meiō|ja|明応}} (1493). == Cultural Properties == [[File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 07.JPG|thumb|Main hall (National Important Cultural Property)]] === Important Cultural Property (Nationally Designated) === * Main Hall *: The main hall, constructed in the {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} style with a cypress bark roof, was first built in the late {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}} in the fifth year of {{ill|Shōwa|ja|正和}} (1316). It was rebuilt during the mid-Muromachi period in the sixth year of {{ill|Eikyō|ja|永享}} (1434), and again in 1493 during Meiō 2. Records of its construction are preserved in inscriptions on ridge beams. The porch underwent repairs in the third year of {{ill|Jōkyō|ja|貞享}} (1686). It features a unique regional style, with the inner sanctuary raised on a higher platform. Reused old materials under the floor suggest the structure was originally a four-bay building. Designated on June 9, 1960. === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Nationally Designated) === * Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine Shinji Dance *: Designated on May 17, 1977. For details, see ''[[Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine Shinji Dance]]''. === Hyogo Prefectural Cultural Property === * Shinji Noh Masks *: Twelve noh masks used for the sacred dance survive. Of these, five masks—black old man, young man, father, old man, and king—are still used in ceremonies today. The remaining seven masks (old man, young man, old woman, prince, etc.) have unclear uses. The shrine precinct features a main hall, a divided worship hall (割拝殿), a long hall (長床), and a dance stage (舞殿), arranged around a central courtyard, preserving an ancient shrine layout. It served as the tutelary shrine of Kamikamogawa Village. The main hall is designated as an [[Important Cultural Property]] of Japan and is recorded to have been constructed in the mid-[[Muromachi period]] during the second year of {{ill|Meiō|ja|明応}} (1493). == Cultural Properties == [[File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 07.JPG|thumb|Main hall (National Important Cultural Property)]] === Important Cultural Property (Nationally Designated) === * Main Hall *: The main hall, constructed in the {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} style with a cypress bark roof, was first built in the late {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}} in the fifth year of {{ill|Shōwa|ja|正和}} (1316). It was rebuilt during the mid-Muromachi period in the sixth year of {{ill|Eikyō|ja|永享}} (1434), and again in 1493 during Meiō 2. Records of its construction are preserved in inscriptions on ridge beams. The porch underwent repairs in the third year of {{ill|Jōkyō|ja|貞享}} (1686). It features a unique regional style, with the inner sanctuary raised on a higher platform. Reused old materials under the floor suggest the structure was originally a four-bay building. Designated on June 9, 1960. === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Nationally Designated) === * Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine Shinji Dance *: Designated on May 17, 1977. For details, see ''[[Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine Shinji Dance]]''. === Hyogo Prefectural Cultural Property === * Shinji Noh Masks *: Twelve noh masks used for the sacred dance survive. Of these, five masks—black old man, young man, father, old man, and king—are still used in ceremonies today. The remaining seven masks (old man, young man, old woman, prince, etc.) have unclear uses. Designated on March 29, 1968. == Gallery == File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 02.JPG|Approach staircase File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 08.JPG|Dance stage (right) and long hall (left) File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 09.JPG|Dance stage == References == * Editorial Committee on Hyogo Prefecture Historical Walks / ed., ''Historical Walks of Hyogo Prefecture, Vol. 2,'' {{ill|Yamakawa Shuppansha|ja|山川出版社}} (2006), pp. 61–62. * On-site explanation board. == Related Articles == {{commonscat|Sumiyoshi-jinja (Kamikamogawa, Kato)}} * {{ill|List of Hyogo Prefectural Cultural Properties|ja|兵庫県指定文化財一覧}} == External Links == * [https://www.kato-kanko.jp/2017/03/sumiyoshi-shrine/ Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine] (Kato City Tourism Association) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Hyogo Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan in Hyogo Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kato, Hyogo]] [[Category:History of Kato, Hyogo]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha shrines in Harima Province]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi Shrines]] [[Category:Muromachi-period architecture]] [[Category:Kami-Kamikawa Sumiyoshi Shrine]][[Category:Kami-Kamikawa Sumiyoshi Shrine]][[Category:Kami-Kamikawa Sumiyoshi Shrine]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[ja:住吉神社 (加東市上鴨川)]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Kami-Kamikawa Sumiyoshi Shrine]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Kami-Kamikawa Sumiyoshi Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kami-Kamikawa Sumiyoshi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kami-Kamikawa Sumiyoshi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kami-Kamikawa Sumiyoshi Shrine}}

The shrine precinct features a main hall, a divided worship hall (割拝殿), a long hall (長床), and a dance stage (舞殿), arranged around a central courtyard, preserving an ancient shrine layout. It served as the tutelary shrine of Kamikamogawa Village. The main hall is designated as an [[Important Cultural Property]] of Japan and is recorded to have been constructed in the mid-[[Muromachi period]] during the second year of {{ill|Meiō|ja|明応}} (1493). == Cultural Properties == [[File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 07.JPG|thumb|Main hall (National Important Cultural Property)]] === Important Cultural Property (Nationally Designated) === * Main Hall *: The main hall, constructed in the {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} style with a cypress bark roof, was first built in the late {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}} in the fifth year of {{ill|Shōwa|ja|正和}} (1316). It was rebuilt during the mid-Muromachi period in the sixth year of {{ill|Eikyō|ja|永享}} (1434), and again in 1493 during Meiō 2. Records of its construction are preserved in inscriptions on ridge beams. The porch underwent repairs in the third year of {{ill|Jōkyō|ja|貞享}} (1686). It features a unique regional style, with the inner sanctuary raised on a higher platform. Reused old materials under the floor suggest the structure was originally a four-bay building. Designated on June 9, 1960. === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Nationally Designated) === * Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine Shinji Dance *: Designated on May 17, 1977. For details, see ''[[Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine Shinji Dance]]''. === Hyogo Prefectural Cultural Property === * Shinji Noh Masks *: Twelve noh masks used for the sacred dance survive. Of these, five masks—black old man, young man, father, old man, and king—are still used in ceremonies today. The remaining seven masks (old man, young man, old woman, prince, etc.) have unclear uses. {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Sumiyoshi Shrine |location=[[Hyogo Prefecture]], [[Kato City]], Kamikamo River 571 | Latitude degrees = 34 | Latitude minutes = 58 | Latitude seconds = 0.3 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 135 | Longitude minutes = 3 | Longitude seconds = 34.4 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | deity=[[Sumiyoshi Sanjin]]
[[Empress Jingū|Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto]] | consecration_year=Unknown | Shrine rank=Shikinaisha (small shrine), Village shrine | Annual festival=Shinji Dance (First Saturday and Sunday of October) | Main hall=[[Sanctuary with nagare-zukuri style]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 03.JPG|300px]]
Torii and divided worship hall | Map=Japan Hyogo }} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Kato City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]]. The enshrined deities are the [[Sumiyoshi Sanjin]] (Kamutsutsuno-o no Mikoto, Nakatsutsuno-o no Mikoto, Sokotsutsuno-o no Mikoto) and [[Empress Jingū|Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha (small shrine)|ja|式内小社}} under the [[Engishiki Jinmyocho]], and its former shrine rank under the {{ill|Modern shrine rank system|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was that of a village shrine. It is also referred to as the '''Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine''' due to its location. == Overview == The founding of the shrine is unknown. The shrine precinct features a main hall, a divided worship hall (割拝殿), a long hall (長床), and a dance stage (舞殿), arranged around a central courtyard, preserving an ancient shrine layout. It served as the tutelary shrine of Kamikamogawa Village. The main hall is designated as an [[Important Cultural Property]] of Japan and is recorded to have been constructed in the mid-[[Muromachi period]] during the second year of {{ill|Meiō|ja|明応}} (1493). == Cultural Properties == [[File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 07.JPG|thumb|Main hall (National Important Cultural Property)]] === Important Cultural Property (Nationally Designated) === * Main Hall *: The main hall, constructed in the {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} style with a cypress bark roof, was first built in the late {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}} in the fifth year of {{ill|Shōwa|ja|正和}} (1316). It was rebuilt during the mid-Muromachi period in the sixth year of {{ill|Eikyō|ja|永享}} (1434), and again in 1493 during Meiō 2. Records of its construction are preserved in inscriptions on ridge beams. The porch underwent repairs in the third year of {{ill|Jōkyō|ja|貞享}} (1686). It features a unique regional style, with the inner sanctuary raised on a higher platform. Reused old materials under the floor suggest the structure was originally a four-bay building. Designated on June 9, 1960. === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Nationally Designated) === * Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine Shinji Dance *: Designated on May 17, 1977. For details, see ''[[Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine Shinji Dance]]''. === Hyogo Prefectural Cultural Property === * Shinji Noh Masks *: Twelve noh masks used for the sacred dance survive. Of these, five masks—black old man, young man, father, old man, and king—are still used in ceremonies today. The remaining seven masks (old man, young man, old woman, prince, etc.) have unclear uses. Designated on March 29, 1968. == Gallery == File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 02.JPG|Approach staircase File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 08.JPG|Dance stage (right) and long hall (left) File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 09.JPG|Dance stage == References == * Editorial Committee on Hyogo Prefecture Historical Walks / ed., ''Historical Walks of Hyogo Prefecture, Vol. 2,'' {{ill|Yamakawa Shuppansha|ja|山川出版社}} (2006), pp. 61–62. * On-site explanation board. == Related Articles == {{commonscat|Sumiyoshi-jinja (Kamikamogawa, Kato)}} * {{ill|List of Hyogo Prefectural Cultural Properties|ja|兵庫県指定文化財一覧}} == External Links == * [https://www.kato-kanko.jp/2017/03/sumiyoshi-shrine/ Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine] (Kato City Tourism Association) {{Shinto horizontal}} The main hall is designated as an [[Important Cultural Property]] of Japan and is recorded to have been constructed in the mid-[[Muromachi period]] during the second year of {{ill|Meiō|ja|明応}} (1493). == Cultural Properties == [[File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 07.JPG|thumb|Main hall (National Important Cultural Property)]] === Important Cultural Property (Nationally Designated) === * Main Hall *: The main hall, constructed in the {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} style with a cypress bark roof, was first built in the late {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}} in the fifth year of {{ill|Shōwa|ja|正和}} (1316). It was rebuilt during the mid-Muromachi period in the sixth year of {{ill|Eikyō|ja|永享}} (1434), and again in 1493 during Meiō 2. Records of its construction are preserved in inscriptions on ridge beams. The porch underwent repairs in the third year of {{ill|Jōkyō|ja|貞享}} (1686). It features a unique regional style, with the inner sanctuary raised on a higher platform. Reused old materials under the floor suggest the structure was originally a four-bay building. Designated on June 9, 1960. === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Nationally Designated) === * Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine Shinji Dance *: Designated on May 17, 1977. For details, see ''[[Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine Shinji Dance]]''. === Hyogo Prefectural Cultural Property === * Shinji Noh Masks *: Twelve noh masks used for the sacred dance survive. Of these, five masks—black old man, young man, father, old man, and king—are still used in ceremonies today. The remaining seven masks (old man, young man, old woman, prince, etc.) have unclear uses. Designated on March 29, 1968. == Gallery == File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 02.JPG|Approach staircase File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 08.JPG|Dance stage (right) and long hall (left) File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 09.JPG|Dance stage == References == * Editorial Committee on Hyogo Prefecture Historical Walks / ed., ''Historical Walks of Hyogo Prefecture, Vol. 2,'' {{ill|Yamakawa Shuppansha|ja|山川出版社}} (2006), pp. 61–62. * On-site explanation board. == Related Articles == {{commonscat|Sumiyoshi-jinja (Kamikamogawa, Kato)}} * {{ill|List of Hyogo Prefectural Cultural Properties|ja|兵庫県指定文化財一覧}} == External Links == * [https://www.kato-kanko.jp/2017/03/sumiyoshi-shrine/ Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine] (Kato City Tourism Association) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Hyogo Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan in Hyogo Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kato, Hyogo]] [[Category:History of Kato, Hyogo]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha shrines in Harima Province]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi Shrines]] [[Category:Muromachi-period architecture]] [[Category:Kami-Kamikawa Sumiyoshi Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[ja:住吉神社 (加東市上鴨川)]] [[sn:Kami-Kamikawa Sumiyoshi Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kami-Kamikawa Sumiyoshi Shrine}}

It features a unique regional style, with the inner sanctuary raised on a higher platform. Reused old materials under the floor suggest the structure was originally a four-bay building. It was rebuilt during the mid-Muromachi period in the sixth year of {{ill|Eikyō|ja|永享|12=draft|12=draft}} (1434), and again in 1493 during Meiō 2. Records of its construction are preserved in inscriptions on ridge beams. The porch underwent repairs in the third year of {{ill|Jōkyō|ja|貞享|12=draft|12=draft}} (1686). It features a unique regional style, with the inner sanctuary raised on a higher platform. Reused old materials under the floor suggest the structure was originally a four-bay building. Designated on June 9, 1960. === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Nationally Designated) === * Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine Shinji Dance *: Designated on May 17, 1977. For details, see ''[[Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine Shinji Dance]]''. === Hyogo Prefectural Cultural Property === * Shinji Noh Masks *: Twelve noh masks used for the sacred dance survive. Of these, five masks—black old man, young man, father, old man, and king—are still used in ceremonies today. The remaining seven masks (old man, young man, old woman, prince, etc.) have unclear uses. Records of its construction are preserved in inscriptions on ridge beams. The porch underwent repairs in the third year of {{ill|Jōkyō|ja|貞享|12=draft|12=draft}} (1686). It features a unique regional style, with the inner sanctuary raised on a higher platform. Reused old materials under the floor suggest the structure was originally a four-bay building. Designated on June 9, 1960. === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Nationally Designated) === * Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine Shinji Dance *: Designated on May 17, 1977. It was rebuilt during the mid-Muromachi period in the sixth year of {{ill|Eikyō|ja|永享|12=draft|12=draft}} (1434), and again in 1493 during Meiō 2. Records of its construction are preserved in inscriptions on ridge beams. The porch underwent repairs in the third year of {{ill|Jōkyō|ja|貞享|12=draft|12=draft}} (1686). It features a unique regional style, with the inner sanctuary raised on a higher platform. Reused old materials under the floor suggest the structure was originally a four-bay building. Designated on June 9, 1960. === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Nationally Designated) === * Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine Shinji Dance *: Designated on May 17, 1977. For details, see ''[[Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine Shinji Dance]]''. === Hyogo Prefectural Cultural Property === * Shinji Noh Masks *: Twelve noh masks used for the sacred dance survive. Of these, five masks—black old man, young man, father, old man, and king—are still used in ceremonies today. The remaining seven masks (old man, young man, old woman, prince, etc.) have unclear uses. Of these, five masks—black old man, young man, father, old man, and king—are still used in ceremonies today. The remaining seven masks (old man, young man, old woman, prince, etc.) have unclear uses. Designated on March 29, 1968. == Gallery == File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 02.JPG|Approach staircase File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 08.JPG|Dance stage (right) and long hall (left) File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 09.JPG|Dance stage == References == * Editorial Committee on Hyogo Prefecture Historical Walks / ed., ''Historical Walks of Hyogo Prefecture, Vol. 2,'' {{ill|Yamakawa Shuppansha|ja|山川出版社}} (2006), pp. 61–62. * On-site explanation board. == Related Articles == {{commonscat|Sumiyoshi-jinja (Kamikamogawa, Kato)}} * {{ill|List of Hyogo Prefectural Cultural Properties|ja|兵庫県指定文化財一覧}} == External Links == * [https://www.kato-kanko.jp/2017/03/sumiyoshi-shrine/ Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine] (Kato City Tourism Association) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Hyogo Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan in Hyogo Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kato, Hyogo]] [[Category:History of Kato, Hyogo]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha shrines in Harima Province]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi Shrines]] [[Category:Muromachi-period architecture]] [[Category:Kami-Kamikawa Sumiyoshi Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:住吉神社 (加東市上鴨川)]] [[sn:Kami-Kamikawa Sumiyoshi Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kami-Kamikawa Sumiyoshi Shrine}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Sumiyoshi Shrine |location=[[Hyogo Prefecture]], [[Kato City]], Kamikamo River 571 | Latitude degrees = 34 | Latitude minutes = 58 | Latitude seconds = 0.3 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 135 | Longitude minutes = 3 | Longitude seconds = 34.4 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | deity=[[Sumiyoshi Sanjin]]
[[Empress Jingū|Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto]] | consecration_year=Unknown | Shrine Rank=Shikinaisha (small shrine), Village shrine | Annual festival=Shinji Dance (First Saturday and Sunday of October) | Main hall=[[Sanctuary with nagare-zukuri style]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 03.JPG|300px]]
Torii and divided worship hall | Map=Japan Hyogo }} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Kato City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]]. The enshrined deities are the [[Sumiyoshi Sanjin]] (Kamutsutsuno-o no Mikoto, Nakatsutsuno-o no Mikoto, Sokotsutsuno-o no Mikoto) and [[Empress Jingū|Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha (small shrine)|ja|式内小社}} under the [[Engishiki Jinmyocho]], and its former shrine Rank under the {{ill|Modern shrine Rank system|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines}} was that of a village shrine. It is also referred to as the '''Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine''' due to its location. == Overview == The founding of the shrine is unknown. The shrine precinct features a main hall, a divided worship hall (割拝殿), a long hall (長床), and a dance stage (舞殿), arranged around a central courtyard, preserving an ancient shrine layout. It served as the tutelary shrine of Kamikamogawa Village. The main hall is designated as an [[Important Cultural Property]] of Japan and is recorded to have been constructed in the mid-[[Muromachi period]] during the second year of {{ill|Meiō|ja|明応}} (1493). == Cultural Properties == [[File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 07.JPG|thumb|Main hall (National Important Cultural Property)]] === Important Cultural Property (Nationally Designated) === * Main Hall *: The main hall, constructed in the {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} style with a cypress bark roof, was first built in the late {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}} in the fifth year of {{ill|Shōwa|ja|正和}} (1316). It was rebuilt during the mid-Muromachi period in the sixth year of {{ill|Eikyō|ja|永享}} (1434), and again in 1493 during Meiō 2. Records of its construction are preserved in inscriptions on ridge beams. The porch underwent repairs in the third year of {{ill|Jōkyō|ja|貞享}} (1686). It features a unique regional style, with the inner sanctuary raised on a higher platform. Reused old materials under the floor suggest the structure was originally a four-bay building. Designated on June 9, 1960. === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Nationally Designated) === * Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine Shinji Dance *: Designated on May 17, 1977. For details, see ''[[Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine Shinji Dance]]''. === Hyogo Prefectural Cultural Property === * Shinji Noh Masks *: Twelve noh masks used for the sacred dance survive. Of these, five masks—black old man, young man, father, old man, and king—are still used in ceremonies today. The remaining seven masks (old man, young man, old woman, prince, etc.) have unclear uses. Designated on March 29, 1968. == Gallery == File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 02.JPG|Approach staircase File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 08.JPG|Dance stage (right) and long hall (left) File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 09.JPG|Dance stage == References == * Editorial Committee on Hyogo Prefecture Historical Walks / ed., ''Historical Walks of Hyogo Prefecture, Vol. 2,'' {{ill|Yamakawa Shuppansha|ja|山川出版社}} (2006), pp. 61–62. * On-site explanation board. == Related Articles == {{commonscat|Sumiyoshi-jinja (Kamikamogawa, Kato)}} * {{ill|List of Hyogo Prefectural Cultural Properties|ja|兵庫県指定文化財一覧}} == External Links == * [https://www.kato-kanko.jp/2017/03/sumiyoshi-shrine/ Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine] (Kato City Tourism Association) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Hyogo Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan in Hyogo Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kato, Hyogo]] [[Category:History of Kato, Hyogo]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha shrines in Harima Province]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi Shrines]] [[Category:Muromachi-period architecture]] [[Category:Kami-Kamikawa Sumiyoshi Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:住吉神社 (加東市上鴨川)]] [[sn:Kami-Kamikawa Sumiyoshi Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kami-Kamikawa Sumiyoshi Shrine}} {{translated page|ja|住吉神社 (加東市上鴨川)|version=80255673|comment=Imported full ja history}}

Designated on March 29, 1968. == Gallery == File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 02.JPG|Approach staircase File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 08.JPG|Dance stage (right) and long hall (left) File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 09.JPG|Dance stage == References == * Editorial Committee on Hyogo Prefecture Historical Walks / ed., ''Historical Walks of Hyogo Prefecture, Vol. 2,'' {{ill|Yamakawa Shuppansha|ja|山川出版社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (2006), pp. 61–62. * On-site explanation board. == Related Articles == {{commonscat|Sumiyoshi-jinja (Kamikamogawa, Kato)}} * {{ill|List of Hyogo Prefectural Cultural Properties|ja|兵庫県指定文化財一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [https://www.kato-kanko.jp/2017/03/sumiyoshi-shrine/ Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine] (Kato City Tourism Association) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|住吉神社 (加東市上鴨川)|version=80255673|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kami-Kamikawa Sumiyoshi Shrine}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Hyogo Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan in Hyogo Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kato, Hyogo]] [[Category:History of Kato, Hyogo]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha shrines in Harima Province]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi Shrines]] [[Category:Muromachi-period architecture]] [[Category:Kami-Kamikawa Sumiyoshi Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:住吉神社 (加東市上鴨川)]] [[sn:Kami-Kamikawa Sumiyoshi Shrine]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Sumiyoshi Shrine |location=[[Hyogo Prefecture]], [[Kato City]], Kamikamo River 571 | Latitude degrees = 34 | Latitude minutes = 58 | Latitude seconds = 0.3 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 135 | Longitude minutes = 3 | Longitude seconds = 34.4 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | deity=[[Sumiyoshi Sanjin]]
[[Empress Jingū|Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto]] | consecration_year=Unknown | Shrine Rank=Shikinaisha (small shrine), Village shrine | Annual festival=Shinji Dance (First Saturday and Sunday of October) | Main hall=[[Sanctuary with nagare-zukuri style]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 03.JPG|300px]]
Torii and divided worship hall | Map=Japan Hyogo }} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Kato City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]]. The enshrined deities are the [[Sumiyoshi Sanjin]] (Kamutsutsuno-o no Mikoto, Nakatsutsuno-o no Mikoto, Sokotsutsuno-o no Mikoto) and [[Empress Jingū|Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha (small shrine)|ja|式内小社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} under the [[Engishiki Jinmyocho]], and its former shrine Rank under the {{ill|Modern shrine Rank system|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was that of a village shrine. It is also referred to as the '''Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine''' due to its location. == Overview == The founding of the shrine is unknown. The shrine precinct features a main hall, a divided worship hall (割拝殿), a long hall (長床), and a dance stage (舞殿), arranged around a central courtyard, preserving an ancient shrine layout. It served as the tutelary shrine of Kamikamogawa Village. The main hall is designated as an [[Important Cultural Property]] of Japan and is recorded to have been constructed in the mid-[[Muromachi period]] during the second year of {{ill|Meiō|ja|明応|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1493). == Cultural Properties == [[File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 07.JPG|thumb|Main hall (National Important Cultural Property)]] === Important Cultural Property (Nationally Designated) === * Main Hall *: The main hall, constructed in the {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} style with a cypress bark roof, was first built in the late {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the fifth year of {{ill|Shōwa|ja|正和|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1316). It was rebuilt during the mid-Muromachi period in the sixth year of {{ill|Eikyō|ja|永享|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1434), and again in 1493 during Meiō 2. Records of its construction are preserved in inscriptions on ridge beams. The porch underwent repairs in the third year of {{ill|Jōkyō|ja|貞享|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1686). It features a unique regional style, with the inner sanctuary raised on a higher platform. Reused old materials under the floor suggest the structure was originally a four-bay building. Designated on June 9, 1960. === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Nationally Designated) === * Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine Shinji Dance *: Designated on May 17, 1977. For details, see ''[[Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine Shinji Dance]]''. === Hyogo Prefectural Cultural Property === * Shinji Noh Masks *: Twelve noh masks used for the sacred dance survive. Of these, five masks—black old man, young man, father, old man, and king—are still used in ceremonies today. The remaining seven masks (old man, young man, old woman, prince, etc.) have unclear uses. Designated on March 29, 1968. == Gallery == File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 02.JPG|Approach staircase File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 08.JPG|Dance stage (right) and long hall (left) File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 09.JPG|Dance stage == References == * Editorial Committee on Hyogo Prefecture Historical Walks / ed., ''Historical Walks of Hyogo Prefecture, Vol. 2,'' {{ill|Yamakawa Shuppansha|ja|山川出版社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (2006), pp. 61–62. * On-site explanation board. == Related Articles == {{commonscat|Sumiyoshi-jinja (Kamikamogawa, Kato)}} * {{ill|List of Hyogo Prefectural Cultural Properties|ja|兵庫県指定文化財一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [https://www.kato-kanko.jp/2017/03/sumiyoshi-shrine/ Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine] (Kato City Tourism Association) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|住吉神社 (加東市上鴨川)|version=80255673|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kami-Kamikawa Sumiyoshi Shrine}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Hyogo Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan in Hyogo Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kato, Hyogo]] [[Category:History of Kato, Hyogo]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha shrines in Harima Province]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi Shrines]] [[Category:Muromachi-period architecture]] [[Category:Kami-Kamikawa Sumiyoshi Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:住吉神社 (加東市上鴨川)]] [[sn:Kami-Kamikawa Sumiyoshi Shrine]]

[[Empress Jingū|Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto]] | consecration_year=Unknown | Shrine Rank=Shikinaisha (small shrine), Village shrine | Annual festival=Shinji Dance (First Saturday and Sunday of October) | Main hall=[[Sanctuary with nagare-zukuri style]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 03.JPG|300px]]
Torii and divided worship hall | Map=Japan Hyogo }} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Kato City]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]. The enshrined deities are the [[Sumiyoshi Sanjin]] (Kamutsutsuno-o no Mikoto, Nakatsutsuno-o no Mikoto, Sokotsutsuno-o no Mikoto) and [[Empress Jingū|Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha (small shrine)|ja|式内小社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha (small shrine)|qq=}} under the [[Engishiki Jinmyocho]], and its former shrine Rank under the {{ill|Modern shrine Rank system|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern shrine Rank system|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=}} was that of a village shrine. It is also referred to as the '''Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine''' due to its location. == Overview == The founding of the shrine is unknown. The shrine precinct features a main hall, a divided worship hall (割拝殿), a long hall (長床), and a dance stage (舞殿), arranged around a central courtyard, preserving an ancient shrine layout. It served as the tutelary shrine of Kamikamogawa Village. The main hall is designated as an [[Important Cultural Property]] of Japan and is recorded to have been constructed in the mid-[[Muromachi period]] during the second year of {{ill|Meiō|ja|明応|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiō|qq=}} (1493). == Cultural Properties == [[File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 07.JPG|thumb|Main hall (National Important Cultural Property)]] === Important Cultural Property (Nationally Designated) === * Main Hall *: The main hall, constructed in the {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagare-zukuri|qq=}} style with a cypress bark roof, was first built in the late {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura period|qq=}} in the fifth year of {{ill|Shōwa|ja|正和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa|qq=}} (1316). It was rebuilt during the mid-Muromachi period in the sixth year of {{ill|Eikyō|ja|永享|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eikyō|qq=}} (1434), and again in 1493 during Meiō 2. Records of its construction are preserved in inscriptions on ridge beams. The porch underwent repairs in the third year of {{ill|Jōkyō|ja|貞享|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōkyō|qq=}} (1686). It features a unique regional style, with the inner sanctuary raised on a higher platform. Reused old materials under the floor suggest the structure was originally a four-bay building. Designated on June 9, 1960. === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Nationally Designated) === * Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine Shinji Dance *: Designated on May 17, 1977. For details, see ''[[Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine Shinji Dance]]''. === Hyogo Prefectural Cultural Property === * Shinji Noh Masks *: Twelve noh masks used for the sacred dance survive. Of these, five masks—black old man, young man, father, old man, and king—are still used in ceremonies today. The remaining seven masks (old man, young man, old woman, prince, etc.) have unclear uses. Designated on March 29, 1968. == Gallery == File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 02.JPG|Approach staircase File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 08.JPG|Dance stage (right) and long hall (left) File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 09.JPG|Dance stage == References == * Editorial Committee on Hyōgo Prefecture Historical Walks / ed., ''Historical Walks of Hyōgo Prefecture, Vol. 2,'' {{ill|Yamakawa Shuppansha|ja|山川出版社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamakawa Shuppansha|qq=}} (2006), pp. 61–62. * On-site explanation board. == Related Articles == {{commonscat|Sumiyoshi-jinja (Kamikamogawa, Kato)}} * {{ill|List of Hyogo Prefectural Cultural Properties|ja|兵庫県指定文化財一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Hyogo Prefectural Cultural Properties|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://www.kato-kanko.jp/2017/03/sumiyoshi-shrine/ Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine] (Kato City Tourism Association) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|住吉神社 (加東市上鴨川)|version=80255673|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kato, Hyogo]] [[Category:History of Kato, Hyogo]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Harima Province]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi Shrines]] [[Category:Muromachi-period architecture]] [[Category:Kami-Kamikawa Sumiyoshi Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:住吉神社 (加東市上鴨川)]] [[sn:Kami-Kamikawa Sumiyoshi Shrine]]

The enshrined deities are the [[Sumiyoshi Sanjin]] (Kamutsutsuno-o no Mikoto, Nakatsutsuno-o no Mikoto, Sokotsutsuno-o no Mikoto) and [[Empress Jingū|Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha (small shrine)|ja|式内小社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha (small shrine)|qq=}} under the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]], and its former shrine Rank under the {{ill|Modern shrine Rank system|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern shrine Rank system|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=}} was that of a village shrine. It is also referred to as the '''Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine''' due to its location. == Overview == The founding of the shrine is unknown. The shrine precinct features a main hall, a divided worship hall (割拝殿), a long hall (長床), and a dance stage (舞殿), arranged around a central courtyard, preserving an ancient shrine layout. It served as the tutelary shrine of Kamikamogawa Village. The main hall is designated as an [[Important Cultural Property]] of Japan and is recorded to have been constructed in the mid-[[Muromachi period]] during the second year of {{ill|Meiō|ja|明応|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiō|qq=}} (1493). == Cultural Properties == [[File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 07.JPG|thumb|Main hall (National Important Cultural Property)]] === Important Cultural Property (Nationally Designated) === * Main Hall *: The main hall, constructed in the {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagare-zukuri|qq=}} style with a cypress bark roof, was first built in the late {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura period|qq=}} in the fifth year of {{ill|Shōwa|ja|正和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa|qq=}} (1316). It was rebuilt during the mid-Muromachi period in the sixth year of {{ill|Eikyō|ja|永享|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eikyō|qq=}} (1434), and again in 1493 during Meiō 2. Records of its construction are preserved in inscriptions on ridge beams. The porch underwent repairs in the third year of {{ill|Jōkyō|ja|貞享|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōkyō|qq=}} (1686). It features a unique regional style, with the inner sanctuary raised on a higher platform. Reused old materials under the floor suggest the structure was originally a four-bay building. Designated on June 9, 1960. === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Nationally Designated) === * Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine Shinji Dance *: Designated on May 17, 1977. For details, see ''[[Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine Shinji Dance]]''. === Hyōgo Prefectural Cultural Property === * Shinji Noh Masks *: Twelve noh masks used for the sacred dance survive. Of these, five masks—black old man, young man, father, old man, and king—are still used in ceremonies today. The remaining seven masks (old man, young man, old woman, prince, etc.) have unclear uses. Designated on March 29, 1968. == Gallery == File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 02.JPG|Approach staircase File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 08.JPG|Dance stage (right) and long hall (left) File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 09.JPG|Dance stage == References == * Editorial Committee on Hyōgo Prefecture Historical Walks / ed., ''Historical Walks of Hyōgo Prefecture, Vol. 2,'' {{ill|Yamakawa Shuppansha|ja|山川出版社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamakawa Shuppansha|qq=}} (2006), pp. 61–62. * On-site explanation board. == Related Articles == {{commonscat|Sumiyoshi-jinja (Kamikamogawa, Kato)}} * {{ill|List of Hyōgo Prefectural Cultural Properties|ja|兵庫県指定文化財一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Hyōgo Prefectural Cultural Properties|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://www.kato-kanko.jp/2017/03/sumiyoshi-shrine/ Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine] (Kato City Tourism Association) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|住吉神社 (加東市上鴨川)|version=80255673|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kato, Hyōgo]] [[Category:History of Kato, Hyōgo]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Harima Province]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi Shrines]] [[Category:Muromachi-period architecture]] [[Category:Kami-Kamikawa Sumiyoshi Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:住吉神社 (加東市上鴨川)]] [[sn:Kami-Kamikawa Sumiyoshi Shrine]]

Designated on March 29, 1968. == Gallery == File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 02.JPG|Approach staircase File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 08.JPG|Dance stage (right) and long hall (left) File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 09.JPG|Dance stage == References == * Editorial Committee on Hyōgo Prefecture Historical Walks / ed., ''Historical Walks of Hyōgo Prefecture, Vol. 2,'' {{ill|Yamakawa Shuppansha|ja|山川出版社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamakawa Shuppansha|qq=}} (2006), pp. 61–62. * On-site explanation board. == Related Articles == {{commonscat|Sumiyoshi-jinja (Kamikamogawa, Kato)}} * {{ill|List of Hyōgo Prefectural Cultural Properties|ja|兵庫県指定文化財一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Hyōgo Prefectural Cultural Properties|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://www.kato-kanko.jp/2017/03/sumiyoshi-shrine/ Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine] (Kato City Tourism Association) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|住吉神社 (加東市上鴨川)|version=80255673|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kato, Hyōgo]] [[Category:History of Kato, Hyōgo]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Harima Province]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi Shrines]] [[Category:Muromachi-period architecture]] [[Category:Kami-Kamikawa Sumiyoshi Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:住吉神社 (加東市上鴨川)]] [[sn:Kami-Kamikawa Sumiyoshi Shrine]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha in Hyōgo Prefecture]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Sumiyoshi Shrine |location=[[Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[Kato City]], Kamikamo River 571 | Latitude degrees = 34 | Latitude minutes = 58 | Latitude seconds = 0.3 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 135 | Longitude minutes = 3 | Longitude seconds = 34.4 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | deity=[[Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi Sanjin]]
[[Empress Jingū|Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto]] | consecration_year=Unknown | Shrine Rank=Shikinaisha (small shrine), Village shrine | Annual festival=Shinji Dance (First Saturday and Sunday of October) | Main hall=[[Sanctuary with nagare-zukuri style]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 03.JPG|300px]]
Torii and divided worship hall | Map=Japan Hyōgo }} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Kato City]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]. The enshrined deities are the [[Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi Sanjin]] (Kamutsutsuno-o no Mikoto, Nakatsutsuno-o no Mikoto, Sokotsutsuno-o no Mikoto) and [[Empress Jingū|Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha (small shrine)|ja|式内小社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha (small shrine)|qq=}} under the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]], and its former shrine Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was that of a village shrine. It is also referred to as the '''Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine''' due to its location. == Overview == The founding of the shrine is unknown. The shrine precinct features a main hall, a divided worship hall (割拝殿), a long hall (長床), and a dance stage (舞殿), arranged around a central courtyard, preserving an ancient shrine layout. It served as the tutelary shrine of Kamikamogawa Village. The main hall is designated as an [[Important Cultural Property]] of Japan and is recorded to have been constructed in the mid-[[Muromachi period]] during the second year of [[:en:Meiō|Meiō]] (1493). == Cultural Properties == [[File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 07.JPG|thumb|Main hall (National Important Cultural Property)]] === Important Cultural Property (Nationally Designated) === * Main Hall *: The main hall, constructed in the [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] style with a cypress bark roof, was first built in the late [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]] in the fifth year of [[:en:Shōwa (1312–1317)|Shōwa]] (1316). It was rebuilt during the mid-Muromachi period in the sixth year of [[:en:Eikyō|Eikyō]] (1434), and again in 1493 during Meiō 2. Records of its construction are preserved in inscriptions on ridge beams. The porch underwent repairs in the third year of [[:en:Jōkyō|Jōkyō]] (1686). It features a unique regional style, with the inner sanctuary raised on a higher platform. Reused old materials under the floor suggest the structure was originally a four-bay building. Designated on June 9, 1960. === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Nationally Designated) === * Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine Shinji Dance *: Designated on May 17, 1977. For details, see ''[[Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine Shinji Dance]]''. === Hyōgo Prefectural Cultural Property === * Shinji Noh Masks *: Twelve noh masks used for the sacred dance survive. Of these, five masks—black old man, young man, father, old man, and king—are still used in ceremonies today. The remaining seven masks (old man, young man, old woman, prince, etc.) have unclear uses. Designated on March 29, 1968. == Gallery == File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 02.JPG|Approach staircase File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 08.JPG|Dance stage (right) and long hall (left) File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 09.JPG|Dance stage == References == * Editorial Committee on Hyōgo Prefecture Historical Walks / ed., ''Historical Walks of Hyōgo Prefecture, Vol. 2,'' {{ill|Yamakawa Shuppansha|ja|山川出版社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamakawa Shuppansha|qq=}} (2006), pp. 61–62. * On-site explanation board. == Related Articles == {{commonscat|Sumiyoshi-jinja (Kamikamogawa, Kato)}} * {{ill|List of Hyōgo Prefectural Cultural Properties|ja|兵庫県指定文化財一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Hyōgo Prefectural Cultural Properties|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://www.kato-kanko.jp/2017/03/sumiyoshi-shrine/ Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine] (Kato City Tourism Association) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|住吉神社 (加東市上鴨川)|version=80255673|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kato, Hyōgo]] [[Category:History of Kato, Hyōgo]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Harima Province]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi Shrines]] [[Category:Muromachi-period architecture]] [[Category:Kami-Kamikawa Sumiyoshi Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:住吉神社 (加東市上鴨川)]] [[sn:Kami-Kamikawa Sumiyoshi Shrine]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]]

Records of its construction are preserved in inscriptions on ridge beams. The porch underwent repairs in the third year of [[:en:Jōkyō|Jōkyō]] (1686). It features a unique regional style, with the inner sanctuary raised on a higher platform. Reused old materials under the floor suggest the structure was originally a four-bay building. Designated on June 9, 1960. === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Nationally Designated) === * Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine Shinji Dance *: Designated on May 17, 1977. For details, see ''[[Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine Shinji Dance]]''. === Hyōgo Prefectural Cultural Property === * Shinji Noh Masks *: Twelve noh masks used for the sacred dance survive. Torii and divided worship hall | Map=Japan Hyōgo }} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Kato City]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]. The enshrined deities are the [[Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi Sanjin]] (Kamutsutsuno-o no Mikoto, Nakatsutsuno-o no Mikoto, Sokotsutsuno-o no Mikoto) and [[Empress Jingū|Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto]]. It is also referred to as the '''Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine''' due to its location. == Overview == The founding of the shrine is unknown. The shrine precinct features a main hall, a divided worship hall (割拝殿), a long hall (長床), and a dance stage (舞殿), arranged around a central courtyard, preserving an ancient shrine layout. It served as the tutelary shrine of Kamikamogawa Village. The main hall is designated as an [[Important Cultural Property]] of Japan and is recorded to have been constructed in the mid-[[Muromachi period]] during the second year of [[:en:Meiō|Meiō]] (1493). == Cultural Properties == [[File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 07.JPG|thumb|Main hall (National Important Cultural Property)]] === Important Cultural Property (Nationally Designated) === * Main Hall *: The main hall, constructed in the [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] style with a cypress bark roof, was first built in the late [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]] in the fifth year of [[:en:Shōwa (1312–1317)|Shōwa]] (1316). It was rebuilt during the mid-Muromachi period in the sixth year of [[:en:Eikyō|Eikyō]] (1434), and again in 1493 during Meiō 2. Records of its construction are preserved in inscriptions on ridge beams. The porch underwent repairs in the third year of [[:en:Jōkyō|Jōkyō]] (1686). It features a unique regional style, with the inner sanctuary raised on a higher platform. Reused old materials under the floor suggest the structure was originally a four-bay building. Designated on June 9, 1960. === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Nationally Designated) === * Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine Shinji Dance *: Designated on May 17, 1977. For details, see ''[[Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine Shinji Dance]]''. === Hyōgo Prefectural Cultural Property === * Shinji Noh Masks *: Twelve noh masks used for the sacred dance survive. Of these, five masks—black old man, young man, father, old man, and king—are still used in ceremonies today. The remaining seven masks (old man, young man, old woman, prince, etc.) have unclear uses. Designated on March 29, 1968. == Gallery == File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 02.JPG|Approach staircase File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 08.JPG|Dance stage (right) and long hall (left) File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 09.JPG|Dance stage == References == * Editorial Committee on Hyōgo Prefecture Historical Walks / ed., ''Historical Walks of Hyōgo Prefecture, Vol. 2,'' {{ill|Yamakawa Shuppansha|ja|山川出版社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamakawa Shuppansha|qq=}} (2006), pp. 61–62. * On-site explanation board. == Related Articles == {{commonscat|Sumiyoshi-jinja (Kamikamogawa, Kato)}} * {{ill|List of Hyōgo Prefectural Cultural Properties|ja|兵庫県指定文化財一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Hyōgo Prefectural Cultural Properties|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://www.kato-kanko.jp/2017/03/sumiyoshi-shrine/ Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine] (Kato City Tourism Association) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|住吉神社 (加東市上鴨川)|version=80255673|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kato, Hyōgo]] [[Category:History of Kato, Hyōgo]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Harima Province]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi Shrines]] [[Category:Muromachi-period architecture]] [[Category:Kami-Kamikawa Sumiyoshi Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:住吉神社 (加東市上鴨川)]] [[sn:Kami-Kamikawa Sumiyoshi Shrine]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:兵庫県の重要文化財]] [[Category:加東市の建築物]] [[Category:加東市の歴史]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]]

The enshrined deities are the [[Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi Sanjin]] (Kamutsutsuno-o no Mikoto, Nakatsutsuno-o no Mikoto, Sokotsutsuno-o no Mikoto) and [[Empress Jingū|Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha (small shrine)|ja|式内小社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha (small shrine)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} under the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]], and its former shrine Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was that of a village shrine. It is also referred to as the '''Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine''' due to its location. == Overview == The founding of the shrine is unknown. The shrine precinct features a main hall, a divided worship hall (割拝殿), a long hall (長床), and a dance stage (舞殿), arranged around a central courtyard, preserving an ancient shrine layout. It served as the tutelary shrine of Kamikamogawa Village. The main hall is designated as an [[Important Cultural Property]] of Japan and is recorded to have been constructed in the mid-[[Muromachi period]] during the second year of [[:en:Meiō|Meiō]] (1493). == Cultural Properties == [[File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 07.JPG|thumb|Main hall (National Important Cultural Property)]] === Important Cultural Property (Nationally Designated) === * Main Hall *: The main hall, constructed in the [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] style with a cypress bark roof, was first built in the late [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]] in the fifth year of [[:en:Shōwa (1312–1317)|Shōwa]] (1316). It was rebuilt during the mid-Muromachi period in the sixth year of [[:en:Eikyō|Eikyō]] (1434), and again in 1493 during Meiō 2. Records of its construction are preserved in inscriptions on ridge beams. The porch underwent repairs in the third year of [[:en:Jōkyō|Jōkyō]] (1686). It features a unique regional style, with the inner sanctuary raised on a higher platform. Reused old materials under the floor suggest the structure was originally a four-bay building. Designated on June 9, 1960. === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Nationally Designated) === * Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine Shinji Dance *: Designated on May 17, 1977. For details, see ''[[Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine Shinji Dance]]''. === Hyōgo Prefectural Cultural Property === * Shinji Noh Masks *: Twelve noh masks used for the sacred dance survive. Of these, five masks—black old man, young man, father, old man, and king—are still used in ceremonies today. The remaining seven masks (old man, young man, old woman, prince, etc.) have unclear uses. Designated on March 29, 1968. == Gallery == File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 02.JPG|Approach staircase File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 08.JPG|Dance stage (right) and long hall (left) File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 09.JPG|Dance stage == References == * Editorial Committee on Hyōgo Prefecture Historical Walks / ed., ''Historical Walks of Hyōgo Prefecture, Vol. 2,'' {{ill|Yamakawa Shuppansha|ja|山川出版社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamakawa Shuppansha|qq=}} (2006), pp. 61–62. * On-site explanation board. == Related Articles == {{commonscat|Sumiyoshi-jinja (Kamikamogawa, Kato)}} * {{ill|List of Hyōgo Prefectural Cultural Properties|ja|兵庫県指定文化財一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Hyōgo Prefectural Cultural Properties|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://www.kato-kanko.jp/2017/03/sumiyoshi-shrine/ Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine] (Kato City Tourism Association) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|住吉神社 (加東市上鴨川)|version=80255673|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Harima Province]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi Shrines]] [[Category:Muromachi-period architecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:住吉神社 (加東市上鴨川)]] [[sn:Kami-Kamikawa Sumiyoshi Shrine]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Hyogo prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Kato, Hyogo]] [[Category:加東市の歴史]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] {{Commons category|Sumiyoshi-jinja (Kamikamogawa, Kato)}}
