{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Sumiyoshi Shrine |location=[[Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[Kato City]], Kamikamo River 571 | Latitude degrees = 34 | Latitude minutes = 58 | Latitude seconds = 0.3 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 135 | Longitude minutes = 3 | Longitude seconds = 34.4 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | deity=[[Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi Sanjin]]
[[Empress Jingū|Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto]] | consecration_year=Unknown | Shrine Rank=Shikinaisha (small shrine), Village shrine | Annual festival=Shinji Dance (First Saturday and Sunday of October) | Main hall=[[Sanctuary with nagare-zukuri style]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 03.JPG|300px]]
Torii and divided worship hall | Map=Japan Hyōgo }} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Kato City]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]. The enshrined deities are the [[Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi Sanjin]] (Kamutsutsuno-o no Mikoto, Nakatsutsuno-o no Mikoto, Sokotsutsuno-o no Mikoto) and [[Empress Jingū|Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha (small shrine)|ja|式内小社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha (small shrine)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} under the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]], and its former shrine Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was that of a village shrine. It is also referred to as the '''Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine''' due to its location. == Overview == The founding of the shrine is unknown. The shrine precinct features a main hall, a divided worship hall (割拝殿), a long hall (長床), and a dance stage (舞殿), arranged around a central courtyard, preserving an ancient shrine layout. It served as the tutelary shrine of Kamikamogawa Village. The main hall is designated as an [[Important Cultural Property]] of Japan and is recorded to have been constructed in the mid-[[Muromachi period]] during the second year of [[:en:Meiō|Meiō]] (1493). == Cultural Properties == [[File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 07.JPG|thumb|Main hall (National Important Cultural Property)]] === Important Cultural Property (Nationally Designated) === * Main Hall *: The main hall, constructed in the [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] style with a cypress bark roof, was first built in the late [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]] in the fifth year of [[:en:Shōwa (1312–1317)|Shōwa]] (1316). It was rebuilt during the mid-Muromachi period in the sixth year of [[:en:Eikyō|Eikyō]] (1434), and again in 1493 during Meiō 2. Records of its construction are preserved in inscriptions on ridge beams. The porch underwent repairs in the third year of [[:en:Jōkyō|Jōkyō]] (1686). It features a unique regional style, with the inner sanctuary raised on a higher platform. Reused old materials under the floor suggest the structure was originally a four-bay building. Designated on June 9, 1960. === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Nationally Designated) === * Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine Shinji Dance *: Designated on May 17, 1977. For details, see ''[[Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine Shinji Dance]]''. === Hyōgo Prefectural Cultural Property === * Shinji Noh Masks *: Twelve noh masks used for the sacred dance survive. Of these, five masks—black old man, young man, father, old man, and king—are still used in ceremonies today. The remaining seven masks (old man, young man, old woman, prince, etc.) have unclear uses. Designated on March 29, 1968. == Gallery == File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 02.JPG|Approach staircase File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 08.JPG|Dance stage (right) and long hall (left) File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 09.JPG|Dance stage == References == * Editorial Committee on Hyōgo Prefecture Historical Walks / ed., ''Historical Walks of Hyōgo Prefecture, Vol. 2,'' {{ill|Yamakawa Shuppansha|ja|山川出版社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamakawa Shuppansha|qq=}} (2006), pp. 61–62. * On-site explanation board. == Related Articles == {{commonscat|Sumiyoshi-jinja (Kamikamogawa, Kato)}} * {{ill|List of Hyōgo Prefectural Cultural Properties|ja|兵庫県指定文化財一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Hyōgo Prefectural Cultural Properties|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://www.kato-kanko.jp/2017/03/sumiyoshi-shrine/ Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine] (Kato City Tourism Association) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|住吉神社 (加東市上鴨川)|version=80255673|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Harima Province]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:住吉神社 (加東市上鴨川)]] [[sn:Kami-Kamikawa Sumiyoshi Shrine]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Hyogo prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Kato, Hyogo]] [[Category:加東市の歴史]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] {{Commons category|Sumiyoshi-jinja (Kamikamogawa, Kato)}}

It was rebuilt during the mid-Muromachi period in the sixth year of [[:en:Eikyō|Eikyō]] (1434), and again in 1493 during Meiō 2. Records of its construction are preserved in inscriptions on ridge beams. The porch underwent repairs in the third year of [[:en:Jōkyō|Jōkyō]] (1686). It features a unique regional style, with the inner sanctuary raised on a higher platform. Reused old materials under the floor suggest the structure was originally a four-bay building. Designated on June 9, 1960. === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Nationally Designated) === * Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine Shinji Dance *: Designated on May 17, 1977. For details, see ''[[Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine Shinji Dance]]''. === Hyōgo Prefectural Cultural Property === * Shinji Noh Masks *: Twelve noh masks used for the sacred dance survive. Of these, five masks—black old man, young man, father, old man, and king—are still used in ceremonies today. The remaining seven masks (old man, young man, old woman, prince, etc.) have unclear uses. Designated on March 29, 1968. == Gallery == File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 02.JPG|Approach staircase File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 08.JPG|Dance stage (right) and long hall (left) File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 09.JPG|Dance stage == References == * Editorial Committee on Hyōgo Prefecture Historical Walks / ed., ''Historical Walks of Hyōgo Prefecture, Vol. 2,'' {{ill|Yamakawa Shuppansha|ja|山川出版社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamakawa Shuppansha|qq=}} (2006), pp. 61–62. * On-site explanation board. == Related Articles == {{commonscat|Sumiyoshi-jinja (Kamikamogawa, Kato)}} * {{ill|List of Hyōgo Prefectural Cultural Properties|ja|兵庫県指定文化財一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Hyōgo Prefectural Cultural Properties|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://www.kato-kanko.jp/2017/03/sumiyoshi-shrine/ Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine] (Kato City Tourism Association) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|住吉神社 (加東市上鴨川)|version=80255673|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Harima Province]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:住吉神社 (加東市上鴨川)]] [[sn:Kami-Kamikawa Sumiyoshi Shrine]] [[Category:Son-sha in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Hyogo prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Kato, Hyogo]] [[Category:加東市の歴史]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] {{Commons category|Sumiyoshi-jinja (Kamikamogawa, Kato)}}

Torii and divided worship hall | Map=Japan Hyōgo }} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Kato City]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. The enshrined deities are the [[Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi Sanjin]] (Kamutsutsuno-o no Mikoto, Nakatsutsuno-o no Mikoto, Sokotsutsuno-o no Mikoto) and [[:en:Empress Jingū|Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto]]. It is also referred to as the '''Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine''' due to its location. == Overview == The founding of the shrine is unknown. The shrine precinct features a main hall, a divided worship hall (割拝殿), a long hall (長床), and a dance stage (舞殿), arranged around a central courtyard, preserving an ancient shrine layout. It served as the tutelary shrine of Kamikamogawa Village. It features a unique regional style, with the inner sanctuary raised on a higher platform. Reused old materials under the floor suggest the structure was originally a four-bay building. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha (small shrine)|ja|式内小社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha (small shrine)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} under the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]], and its former shrine Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was that of a village shrine. It is also referred to as the '''Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine''' due to its location. == Overview == The founding of the shrine is unknown. The shrine precinct features a main hall, a divided worship hall (割拝殿), a long hall (長床), and a dance stage (舞殿), arranged around a central courtyard, preserving an ancient shrine layout. It served as the tutelary shrine of Kamikamogawa Village. The main hall is designated as an [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property (Japan)]] of Japan and is recorded to have been constructed in the mid-[[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] during the second year of [[:en:Meiō|Meiō]] (1493). == Cultural Properties == [[File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 07.JPG|thumb|Main hall (National Important Cultural Property)]] === Important Cultural Property (Nationally Designated) === * Main Hall *: The main hall, constructed in the [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] style with a cypress bark roof, was first built in the late [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]] in the fifth year of [[:en:Shōwa (1312–1317)|Shōwa]] (1316). It was rebuilt during the mid-Muromachi period in the sixth year of [[:en:Eikyō|Eikyō]] (1434), and again in 1493 during Meiō 2. Records of its construction are preserved in inscriptions on ridge beams. The porch underwent repairs in the third year of [[:en:Jōkyō|Jōkyō]] (1686). It features a unique regional style, with the inner sanctuary raised on a higher platform. Reused old materials under the floor suggest the structure was originally a four-bay building. Designated on June 9, 1960. === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Nationally Designated) === * Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine Shinji Dance *: Designated on May 17, 1977. For details, see ''[[Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine Shinji Dance]]''. === Hyōgo Prefectural Cultural Property === * Shinji Noh Masks *: Twelve noh masks used for the sacred dance survive. Of these, five masks—black old man, young man, father, old man, and king—are still used in ceremonies today. The remaining seven masks (old man, young man, old woman, prince, etc.) have unclear uses. Designated on March 29, 1968. == Gallery == File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 02.JPG|Approach staircase File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 08.JPG|Dance stage (right) and long hall (left) File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 09.JPG|Dance stage == References == * Editorial Committee on Hyōgo Prefecture Historical Walks / ed., ''Historical Walks of Hyōgo Prefecture, Vol. 2,'' {{ill|Yamakawa Shuppansha|ja|山川出版社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamakawa Shuppansha|qq=}} (2006), pp. 61–62. * On-site explanation board. == Related Articles == {{commonscat|Sumiyoshi-jinja (Kamikamogawa, Kato)}} * {{ill|List of Hyōgo Prefectural Cultural Properties|ja|兵庫県指定文化財一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Hyōgo Prefectural Cultural Properties|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://www.kato-kanko.jp/2017/03/sumiyoshi-shrine/ Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine] (Kato City Tourism Association) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|住吉神社 (加東市上鴨川)|version=80255673|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Harima Province]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:住吉神社 (加東市上鴨川)]] [[sn:Kami-Kamikawa Sumiyoshi Shrine]] [[Category:Son-sha in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Hyogo prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Kato, Hyogo]] [[Category:Municipal History of Katō]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] {{Commons category|Sumiyoshi-jinja (Kamikamogawa, Kato)}}

Designated on June 9, 1960. === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Nationally Designated) === * Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine Shinji Dance *: Designated on May 17, 1977. For details, see ''[[Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine Shinji Dance]]''. === Hyōgo Prefectural Cultural Property === * Shinji Noh Masks *: Twelve noh masks used for the sacred dance survive. Of these, five masks—black old man, young man, father, old man, and king—are still used in ceremonies today. The remaining seven masks (old man, young man, old woman, prince, etc.) have unclear uses. Designated on March 29, 1968.

'''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Kato City]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. The enshrined deities are the [[Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi Sanjin]] (Kamutsutsuno-o no Mikoto, Nakatsutsuno-o no Mikoto, Sokotsutsuno-o no Mikoto) and [[:en:Empress Jingū|Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha (small shrine)|ja|式内小社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha (small shrine)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11064932}} under the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]], and its former shrine Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was that of a village shrine. It is also referred to as the '''Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine''' due to its location.
== Overview == The founding of the shrine is unknown.
The shrine precinct features a main hall, a divided worship hall (割拝殿), a long hall (長床), and a dance stage (舞殿), arranged around a central courtyard, preserving an ancient shrine layout.
It served as the tutelary shrine of Kamikamogawa Village. The main hall is designated as an [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property (Japan)]] of Japan and is recorded to have been constructed in the mid-[[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] during the second year of [[:en:Meiō|Meiō]] (1493).
== Cultural Properties == [[File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 07.JPG|thumb|Main hall (National Important Cultural Property)]] === Important Cultural Property (Nationally Designated) === * Main Hall *: The main hall, constructed in the [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] style with a cypress bark roof, was first built in the late [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]] in the fifth year of [[:en:Shōwa (1312–1317)|Shōwa]] (1316). It was rebuilt during the mid-Muromachi period in the sixth year of [[:en:Eikyō|Eikyō]] (1434), and again in 1493 during Meiō 2. Records of its construction are preserved in inscriptions on ridge beams. The porch underwent repairs in the third year of [[:en:Jōkyō|Jōkyō]] (1686). It features a unique regional style, with the inner sanctuary raised on a higher platform. Reused old materials under the floor suggest the structure was originally a four-bay building. Designated on June 9, 1960. === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Nationally Designated) === * Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine Shinji Dance *: Designated on May 17, 1977. For details, see ''[[Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine Shinji Dance]]''. === Hyōgo Prefectural Cultural Property === * Shinji Noh Masks *: Twelve noh masks used for the sacred dance survive. Of these, five masks—black old man, young man, father, old man, and king—are still used in ceremonies today. The remaining seven masks (old man, young man, old woman, prince, etc.) have unclear uses. Designated on March 29, 1968.

The enshrined deities are the [[Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi Sanjin]] (Kamutsutsuno-o no Mikoto, Nakatsutsuno-o no Mikoto, Sokotsutsuno-o no Mikoto) and [[:en:Empress Jingū|Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha (small shrine)|ja|式内小社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha (small shrine)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11064932}} under the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]], and its former shrine Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was that of a village shrine. It is also referred to as the '''Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine''' due to its location.
== Overview == The founding of the shrine is unknown.
The shrine precinct features a main hall, a divided worship hall (割拝殿), a long hall (長床), and a dance stage (舞殿), arranged around a central courtyard, preserving an ancient shrine layout.
It served as the tutelary shrine of Kamikamogawa Village. The main hall is designated as an [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property (Japan)]] of Japan and is recorded to have been constructed in the mid-[[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] during the second year of [[:en:Meiō|Meiō]] (1493).

It was rebuilt during the mid-Muromachi period in the sixth year of [[:en:Eikyō|Eikyō]] (1434), and again in 1493 during Meiō 2. Records of its construction are preserved in inscriptions on ridge beams. The porch underwent repairs in the third year of [[:en:Jōkyō|Jōkyō]] (1686). It features a unique regional style, with the inner sanctuary raised on a higher platform. Reused old materials under the floor suggest the structure was originally a four-bay building. Of these, five masks—black old man, young man, father, old man, and king—are still used in ceremonies today. The remaining seven masks (old man, young man, old woman, prince, etc.) have unclear uses. <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Sumiyoshi-jinja (Kamikamogawa, Kato)]][[ja:住吉神社 (加東市上鴨川)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11381860]] Wikidata name: Sumiyoshi-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11381860]] Wikidata name: Sumiyoshi-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Sumiyoshi Shrine |location=[[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[Kato City]], Kamikamo River 571 | Latitude degrees = 34 | Latitude minutes = 58 | Latitude seconds = 0.3 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 135 | Longitude minutes = 3 | Longitude seconds = 34.4 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | deity={{ill|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803}}; {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|WD=Q1114013}} | consecration_year=Unknown | Shrine Rank=Shikinaisha (small shrine), Village shrine | Annual festival=Shinji Dance (First Saturday and Sunday of October) | Main hall=[[Sanctuary with nagare-zukuri style]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 03.JPG | Map=Japan Hyōgo | location = {{ill|Kato|WD=Q877164}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.96675|N|135.059556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Kato City]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. The enshrined deities are the [[Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi Sanjin]] (Kamutsutsuno-o no Mikoto, Nakatsutsuno-o no Mikoto, Sokotsutsuno-o no Mikoto) and [[:en:Empress Jingū|Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha (small shrine)|ja|式内小社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha (small shrine)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11064932}} under the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]], and its former shrine Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was that of a village shrine. It is also referred to as the '''Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine''' due to its location.
== Overview == The founding of the shrine is unknown.
The shrine precinct features a main hall, a divided worship hall (割拝殿), a long hall (長床), and a dance stage (舞殿), arranged around a central courtyard, preserving an ancient shrine layout.
It served as the tutelary shrine of Kamikamogawa Village. The main hall is designated as an [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property (Japan)]] of Japan and is recorded to have been constructed in the mid-[[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] during the second year of [[:en:Meiō|Meiō]] (1493).
== Cultural Properties == [[File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 07.JPG|thumb|Main hall (National Important Cultural Property)]] === Important Cultural Property (Nationally Designated) === * Main Hall *: The main hall, constructed in the [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] style with a cypress bark roof, was first built in the late [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]] in the fifth year of [[:en:Shōwa (1312–1317)|Shōwa]] (1316). It was rebuilt during the mid-Muromachi period in the sixth year of [[:en:Eikyō|Eikyō]] (1434), and again in 1493 during Meiō 2. Records of its construction are preserved in inscriptions on ridge beams. The porch underwent repairs in the third year of [[:en:Jōkyō|Jōkyō]] (1686). It features a unique regional style, with the inner sanctuary raised on a higher platform. Reused old materials under the floor suggest the structure was originally a four-bay building. Designated on June 9, 1960. === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Nationally Designated) === * Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine Shinji Dance *: Designated on May 17, 1977. For details, see ''[[Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine Shinji Dance]]''. === Hyōgo Prefectural Cultural Property === * Shinji Noh Masks *: Twelve noh masks used for the sacred dance survive. Of these, five masks—black old man, young man, father, old man, and king—are still used in ceremonies today. The remaining seven masks (old man, young man, old woman, prince, etc.) have unclear uses. Designated on March 29, 1968.

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Sumiyoshi Shrine |location=[[Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[Kato City]], Kamikamo River 571 | Latitude degrees = 34 | Latitude minutes = 58 | Latitude seconds = 0.3 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 135 | Longitude minutes = 3 | Longitude seconds = 34.4 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | deity=[[Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi Sanjin]]
[[Empress Jingū|Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto]] | consecration_year=Unknown | Shrine Rank=Shikinaisha (small shrine), Village shrine | Annual festival=Shinji Dance (First Saturday and Sunday of October) | Main hall=[[Sanctuary with nagare-zukuri style]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 03.JPG|300px]]
Torii and divided worship hall | Map=Japan Hyōgo }} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Kato City]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]. The enshrined deities are the [[Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi Sanjin]] (Kamutsutsuno-o no Mikoto, Nakatsutsuno-o no Mikoto, Sokotsutsuno-o no Mikoto) and [[Empress Jingū|Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha (small shrine)|ja|式内小社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha (small shrine)|qq=}} under the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]], and its former shrine Rank under the {{ill|Modern shrine Rank system|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern shrine Rank system|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=}} was that of a village shrine. It is also referred to as the '''Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine''' due to its location. == Overview == The founding of the shrine is unknown. The shrine precinct features a main hall, a divided worship hall (割拝殿), a long hall (長床), and a dance stage (舞殿), arranged around a central courtyard, preserving an ancient shrine layout. It served as the tutelary shrine of Kamikamogawa Village. The main hall is designated as an [[Important Cultural Property]] of Japan and is recorded to have been constructed in the mid-[[Muromachi period]] during the second year of {{ill|Meiō|ja|明応|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiō|qq=}} (1493). == Cultural Properties == [[File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 07.JPG|thumb|Main hall (National Important Cultural Property)]] === Important Cultural Property (Nationally Designated) === * Main Hall *: The main hall, constructed in the {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagare-zukuri|qq=}} style with a cypress bark roof, was first built in the late {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura period|qq=}} in the fifth year of {{ill|Shōwa|ja|正和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa|qq=}} (1316). It was rebuilt during the mid-Muromachi period in the sixth year of {{ill|Eikyō|ja|永享|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eikyō|qq=}} (1434), and again in 1493 during Meiō 2. Records of its construction are preserved in inscriptions on ridge beams. The porch underwent repairs in the third year of {{ill|Jōkyō|ja|貞享|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōkyō|qq=}} (1686). It features a unique regional style, with the inner sanctuary raised on a higher platform. Reused old materials under the floor suggest the structure was originally a four-bay building. Designated on June 9, 1960. === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Nationally Designated) === * Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine Shinji Dance *: Designated on May 17, 1977. For details, see ''[[Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine Shinji Dance]]''. === Hyōgo Prefectural Cultural Property === * Shinji Noh Masks *: Twelve noh masks used for the sacred dance survive. Of these, five masks—black old man, young man, father, old man, and king—are still used in ceremonies today. The remaining seven masks (old man, young man, old woman, prince, etc.) have unclear uses. Designated on March 29, 1968. == Gallery == File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 02.JPG|Approach staircase File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 08.JPG|Dance stage (right) and long hall (left) File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 09.JPG|Dance stage == References == * Editorial Committee on Hyōgo Prefecture Historical Walks / ed., ''Historical Walks of Hyōgo Prefecture, Vol. 2,'' {{ill|Yamakawa Shuppansha|ja|山川出版社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamakawa Shuppansha|qq=}} (2006), pp. 61–62. * On-site explanation board. == Related Articles == {{commonscat|Sumiyoshi-jinja (Kamikamogawa, Kato)}} * {{ill|List of Hyōgo Prefectural Cultural Properties|ja|兵庫県指定文化財一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Hyōgo Prefectural Cultural Properties|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://www.kato-kanko.jp/2017/03/sumiyoshi-shrine/ Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine] (Kato City Tourism Association) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|住吉神社 (加東市上鴨川)|version=80255673|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kato, Hyōgo]] [[Category:History of Kato, Hyōgo]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Harima Province]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi Shrines]] [[Category:Muromachi-period architecture]] [[Category:Kami-Kamikawa Sumiyoshi Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:pages that were shrank]] }} [[ja:住吉神社 (加東市上鴨川)]] [[sn:Kami-Kamikawa Sumiyoshi Shrine]]

[[Empress Jingū|Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto]] | consecration_year=Unknown | Shrine Rank=Shikinaisha (small shrine), Village shrine | Annual festival=Shinji Dance (First Saturday and Sunday of October) | Main hall=[[Sanctuary with nagare-zukuri style]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 03.JPG|300px]]
Torii and divided worship hall | Map=Japan Hyōgo }} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Kato City]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]. The enshrined deities are the [[Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi Sanjin]] (Kamutsutsuno-o no Mikoto, Nakatsutsuno-o no Mikoto, Sokotsutsuno-o no Mikoto) and [[Empress Jingū|Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha (small shrine)|ja|式内小社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha (small shrine)|qq=}} under the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]], and its former shrine Rank under the {{ill|Modern shrine Rank system|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern shrine Rank system|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=}} was that of a village shrine. It is also referred to as the '''Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine''' due to its location. == Overview == The founding of the shrine is unknown. The shrine precinct features a main hall, a divided worship hall (割拝殿), a long hall (長床), and a dance stage (舞殿), arranged around a central courtyard, preserving an ancient shrine layout. It served as the tutelary shrine of Kamikamogawa Village. The main hall is designated as an [[Important Cultural Property]] of Japan and is recorded to have been constructed in the mid-[[Muromachi period]] during the second year of {{ill|Meiō|ja|明応|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiō|qq=}} (1493). == Cultural Properties == [[File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 07.JPG|thumb|Main hall (National Important Cultural Property)]] === Important Cultural Property (Nationally Designated) === * Main Hall *: The main hall, constructed in the {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagare-zukuri|qq=}} style with a cypress bark roof, was first built in the late {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura period|qq=}} in the fifth year of {{ill|Shōwa|ja|正和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa|qq=}} (1316). It was rebuilt during the mid-Muromachi period in the sixth year of {{ill|Eikyō|ja|永享|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eikyō|qq=}} (1434), and again in 1493 during Meiō 2. Records of its construction are preserved in inscriptions on ridge beams. The porch underwent repairs in the third year of {{ill|Jōkyō|ja|貞享|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōkyō|qq=}} (1686). It features a unique regional style, with the inner sanctuary raised on a higher platform. Reused old materials under the floor suggest the structure was originally a four-bay building. Designated on June 9, 1960. === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Nationally Designated) === * Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine Shinji Dance *: Designated on May 17, 1977. For details, see ''[[Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine Shinji Dance]]''. === Hyōgo Prefectural Cultural Property === * Shinji Noh Masks *: Twelve noh masks used for the sacred dance survive. Of these, five masks—black old man, young man, father, old man, and king—are still used in ceremonies today. The remaining seven masks (old man, young man, old woman, prince, etc.) have unclear uses. Designated on March 29, 1968. == Gallery == File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 02.JPG|Approach staircase File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 08.JPG|Dance stage (right) and long hall (left) File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 09.JPG|Dance stage == References == * Editorial Committee on Hyōgo Prefecture Historical Walks / ed., ''Historical Walks of Hyōgo Prefecture, Vol. 2,'' {{ill|Yamakawa Shuppansha|ja|山川出版社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamakawa Shuppansha|qq=}} (2006), pp. 61–62. * On-site explanation board. == Related Articles == {{commonscat|Sumiyoshi-jinja (Kamikamogawa, Kato)}} * {{ill|List of Hyōgo Prefectural Cultural Properties|ja|兵庫県指定文化財一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Hyōgo Prefectural Cultural Properties|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://www.kato-kanko.jp/2017/03/sumiyoshi-shrine/ Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine] (Kato City Tourism Association) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|住吉神社 (加東市上鴨川)|version=80255673|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kato, Hyōgo]] [[Category:History of Kato, Hyōgo]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Harima Province]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi Shrines]] [[Category:Muromachi-period architecture]] [[Category:Kami-Kamikawa Sumiyoshi Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:pages that were shrank]] }} [[ja:住吉神社 (加東市上鴨川)]] [[sn:Kami-Kamikawa Sumiyoshi Shrine]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

Designated on March 29, 1968. == Gallery == File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 02.JPG|Approach staircase File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 08.JPG|Dance stage (right) and long hall (left) File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 09.JPG|Dance stage == References == * Editorial Committee on Hyōgo Prefecture Historical Walks / ed., ''Historical Walks of Hyōgo Prefecture, Vol. 2,'' {{ill|Yamakawa Shuppansha|ja|山川出版社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamakawa Shuppansha|qq=}} (2006), pp. 61–62. * On-site explanation board. == Related Articles == {{commonscat|Sumiyoshi-jinja (Kamikamogawa, Kato)}} * {{ill|List of Hyōgo Prefectural Cultural Properties|ja|兵庫県指定文化財一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Hyōgo Prefectural Cultural Properties|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://www.kato-kanko.jp/2017/03/sumiyoshi-shrine/ Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine] (Kato City Tourism Association) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|住吉神社 (加東市上鴨川)|version=80255673|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kato, Hyōgo]] [[Category:History of Kato, Hyōgo]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Harima Province]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi Shrines]] [[Category:Muromachi-period architecture]] [[Category:Kami-Kamikawa Sumiyoshi Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:pages that were shrank]] }} [[ja:住吉神社 (加東市上鴨川)]] [[sn:Kami-Kamikawa Sumiyoshi Shrine]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Sumiyoshi Shrine |location=[[Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[Kato City]], Kamikamo River 571 | Latitude degrees = 34 | Latitude minutes = 58 | Latitude seconds = 0.3 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 135 | Longitude minutes = 3 | Longitude seconds = 34.4 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | deity=[[Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi Sanjin]]
[[Empress Jingū|Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto]] | consecration_year=Unknown | Shrine Rank=Shikinaisha (small shrine), Village shrine | Annual festival=Shinji Dance (First Saturday and Sunday of October) | Main hall=[[Sanctuary with nagare-zukuri style]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 03.JPG|300px]]
Torii and divided worship hall | Map=Japan Hyōgo }} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Kato City]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]. The enshrined deities are the [[Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi Sanjin]] (Kamutsutsuno-o no Mikoto, Nakatsutsuno-o no Mikoto, Sokotsutsuno-o no Mikoto) and [[Empress Jingū|Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha (small shrine)|ja|式内小社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha (small shrine)|qq=}} under the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]], and its former shrine Rank under the {{ill|Modern shrine Rank system|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern shrine Rank system|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=}} was that of a village shrine. It is also referred to as the '''Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine''' due to its location. == Overview == The founding of the shrine is unknown. The shrine precinct features a main hall, a divided worship hall (割拝殿), a long hall (長床), and a dance stage (舞殿), arranged around a central courtyard, preserving an ancient shrine layout. It served as the tutelary shrine of Kamikamogawa Village. The main hall is designated as an [[Important Cultural Property]] of Japan and is recorded to have been constructed in the mid-[[Muromachi period]] during the second year of {{ill|Meiō|ja|明応|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiō|qq=}} (1493). == Cultural Properties == [[File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 07.JPG|thumb|Main hall (National Important Cultural Property)]] === Important Cultural Property (Nationally Designated) === * Main Hall *: The main hall, constructed in the {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagare-zukuri|qq=}} style with a cypress bark roof, was first built in the late {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura period|qq=}} in the fifth year of {{ill|Shōwa|ja|正和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa|qq=}} (1316). It was rebuilt during the mid-Muromachi period in the sixth year of {{ill|Eikyō|ja|永享|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eikyō|qq=}} (1434), and again in 1493 during Meiō 2. Records of its construction are preserved in inscriptions on ridge beams. The porch underwent repairs in the third year of {{ill|Jōkyō|ja|貞享|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōkyō|qq=}} (1686). It features a unique regional style, with the inner sanctuary raised on a higher platform. Reused old materials under the floor suggest the structure was originally a four-bay building. Designated on June 9, 1960. === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Nationally Designated) === * Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine Shinji Dance *: Designated on May 17, 1977. For details, see ''[[Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine Shinji Dance]]''. === Hyōgo Prefectural Cultural Property === * Shinji Noh Masks *: Twelve noh masks used for the sacred dance survive. Of these, five masks—black old man, young man, father, old man, and king—are still used in ceremonies today. The remaining seven masks (old man, young man, old woman, prince, etc.) have unclear uses. Designated on March 29, 1968. == Gallery == File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 02.JPG|Approach staircase File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 08.JPG|Dance stage (right) and long hall (left) File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 09.JPG|Dance stage == References == * Editorial Committee on Hyōgo Prefecture Historical Walks / ed., ''Historical Walks of Hyōgo Prefecture, Vol. 2,'' {{ill|Yamakawa Shuppansha|ja|山川出版社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamakawa Shuppansha|qq=}} (2006), pp. 61–62. * On-site explanation board. == Related Articles == {{commonscat|Sumiyoshi-jinja (Kamikamogawa, Kato)}} * {{ill|List of Hyōgo Prefectural Cultural Properties|ja|兵庫県指定文化財一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Hyōgo Prefectural Cultural Properties|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://www.kato-kanko.jp/2017/03/sumiyoshi-shrine/ Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine] (Kato City Tourism Association) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|住吉神社 (加東市上鴨川)|version=80255673|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kato, Hyōgo]] [[Category:History of Kato, Hyōgo]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Harima Province]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi Shrines]] [[Category:Muromachi-period architecture]] [[Category:Kami-Kamikawa Sumiyoshi Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Pages that were shrank]] [[Category:Pages that were shrank 2]] }} [[ja:住吉神社 (加東市上鴨川)]] [[sn:Kami-Kamikawa Sumiyoshi Shrine]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Sumiyoshi Shrine |location=[[Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[Kato City]], Kamikamo River 571 | Latitude degrees = 34 | Latitude minutes = 58 | Latitude seconds = 0.3 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 135 | Longitude minutes = 3 | Longitude seconds = 34.4 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | deity=[[Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi Sanjin]]
[[Empress Jingū|Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto]] | consecration_year=Unknown | Shrine Rank=Shikinaisha (small shrine), Village shrine | Annual festival=Shinji Dance (First Saturday and Sunday of October) | Main hall=[[Sanctuary with nagare-zukuri style]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 03.JPG|300px]]
Torii and divided worship hall | Map=Japan Hyōgo }} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Kato City]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]. The enshrined deities are the [[Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi Sanjin]] (Kamutsutsuno-o no Mikoto, Nakatsutsuno-o no Mikoto, Sokotsutsuno-o no Mikoto) and [[Empress Jingū|Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha (small shrine)|ja|式内小社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha (small shrine)|qq=}} under the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]], and its former shrine Rank under the {{ill|Modern shrine Rank system|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern shrine Rank system|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=}} was that of a village shrine. It is also referred to as the '''Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine''' due to its location. == Overview == The founding of the shrine is unknown. The shrine precinct features a main hall, a divided worship hall (割拝殿), a long hall (長床), and a dance stage (舞殿), arranged around a central courtyard, preserving an ancient shrine layout. It served as the tutelary shrine of Kamikamogawa Village. The main hall is designated as an [[Important Cultural Property]] of Japan and is recorded to have been constructed in the mid-[[Muromachi period]] during the second year of {{ill|Meiō|ja|明応|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiō|qq=}} (1493). == Cultural Properties == [[File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 07.JPG|thumb|Main hall (National Important Cultural Property)]] === Important Cultural Property (Nationally Designated) === * Main Hall *: The main hall, constructed in the {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagare-zukuri|qq=}} style with a cypress bark roof, was first built in the late {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura period|qq=}} in the fifth year of {{ill|Shōwa|ja|正和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa|qq=}} (1316). It was rebuilt during the mid-Muromachi period in the sixth year of {{ill|Eikyō|ja|永享|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eikyō|qq=}} (1434), and again in 1493 during Meiō 2. Records of its construction are preserved in inscriptions on ridge beams. The porch underwent repairs in the third year of {{ill|Jōkyō|ja|貞享|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōkyō|qq=}} (1686). It features a unique regional style, with the inner sanctuary raised on a higher platform. Reused old materials under the floor suggest the structure was originally a four-bay building. Designated on June 9, 1960. === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Nationally Designated) === * Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine Shinji Dance *: Designated on May 17, 1977. For details, see ''[[Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine Shinji Dance]]''. === Hyōgo Prefectural Cultural Property === * Shinji Noh Masks *: Twelve noh masks used for the sacred dance survive. Of these, five masks—black old man, young man, father, old man, and king—are still used in ceremonies today. The remaining seven masks (old man, young man, old woman, prince, etc.) have unclear uses. Designated on March 29, 1968. == Gallery == File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 02.JPG|Approach staircase File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 08.JPG|Dance stage (right) and long hall (left) File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 09.JPG|Dance stage == References == * Editorial Committee on Hyōgo Prefecture Historical Walks / ed., ''Historical Walks of Hyōgo Prefecture, Vol. 2,'' {{ill|Yamakawa Shuppansha|ja|山川出版社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamakawa Shuppansha|qq=}} (2006), pp. 61–62. * On-site explanation board. == Related Articles == {{commonscat|Sumiyoshi-jinja (Kamikamogawa, Kato)}} * {{ill|List of Hyōgo Prefectural Cultural Properties|ja|兵庫県指定文化財一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Hyōgo Prefectural Cultural Properties|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://www.kato-kanko.jp/2017/03/sumiyoshi-shrine/ Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine] (Kato City Tourism Association) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|住吉神社 (加東市上鴨川)|version=80255673|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kato, Hyōgo]] [[Category:History of Kato, Hyōgo]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Harima Province]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi Shrines]] [[Category:Muromachi-period architecture]] [[Category:Kami-Kamikawa Sumiyoshi Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Pages that were shrank]] }} [[ja:住吉神社 (加東市上鴨川)]] [[sn:Kami-Kamikawa Sumiyoshi Shrine]] == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Kami-Kamikawa Sumiyoshi Shrine (Q11381860)''' is a shrine in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. It is located in [[Harima Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | RONSHA || {{ill|Kami-Kamikawa Sumiyoshi Shrine|lt=Sumiyoshi Shrine|ja|住吉神社 (加東市上鴨川)|commons|Category:Sumiyoshi-jinja (Kamikamogawa, Kato)|WD=Q11381860}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized}}[[Category:Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)]] || — || — || {{ill|Sumiyoshi Shrine|lt=Sumiyoshi Shrine|WD=Q135041176}}; {{ill|Sumiyoshi Shrine|lt=Sumiyoshi Shrine|WD=Q135041176}} || {{coord|34.966752|135.059517|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=183049 DB] |} [[Category:Shikinaisha in Harima Province]] [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] {{wikidata link|Q11381860}}

[[commons:Category:Sumiyoshi-jinja (Kamikamogawa, Kato)]][[ja:住吉神社 (加東市上鴨川)]] [[commons:Category:Sumiyoshi-jinja (Kamikamogawa, Kato)]][[ja:住吉神社 (加東市上鴨川)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Sumiyoshi Shrine |location=[[Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[Kato City]], Kamikamo River 571 | Latitude degrees = 34 | Latitude minutes = 58 | Latitude seconds = 0.3 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 135 | Longitude minutes = 3 | Longitude seconds = 34.4 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | deity=[[Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi Sanjin]]
[[Empress Jingū|Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto]] | consecration_year=Unknown | Shrine Rank=Shikinaisha (small shrine), Village shrine | Annual festival=Shinji Dance (First Saturday and Sunday of October) | Main hall=[[Sanctuary with nagare-zukuri style]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 03.JPG|300px]]
Torii and divided worship hall | Map=Japan Hyōgo }} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Kato City]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]. The enshrined deities are the [[Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi Sanjin]] (Kamutsutsuno-o no Mikoto, Nakatsutsuno-o no Mikoto, Sokotsutsuno-o no Mikoto) and [[Empress Jingū|Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha (small shrine)|ja|式内小社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha (small shrine)|qq=}} under the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]], and its former shrine Rank under the {{ill|Modern shrine Rank system|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern shrine Rank system|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=}} was that of a village shrine. It is also referred to as the '''Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine''' due to its location. == Overview == The founding of the shrine is unknown. The shrine precinct features a main hall, a divided worship hall (割拝殿), a long hall (長床), and a dance stage (舞殿), arranged around a central courtyard, preserving an ancient shrine layout. It served as the tutelary shrine of Kamikamogawa Village. The main hall is designated as an [[Important Cultural Property]] of Japan and is recorded to have been constructed in the mid-[[Muromachi period]] during the second year of {{ill|Meiō|ja|明応|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiō|qq=}} (1493). == Cultural Properties == [[File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 07.JPG|thumb|Main hall (National Important Cultural Property)]] === Important Cultural Property (Nationally Designated) === * Main Hall *: The main hall, constructed in the {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagare-zukuri|qq=}} style with a cypress bark roof, was first built in the late {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura period|qq=}} in the fifth year of {{ill|Shōwa|ja|正和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa|qq=}} (1316). It was rebuilt during the mid-Muromachi period in the sixth year of {{ill|Eikyō|ja|永享|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eikyō|qq=}} (1434), and again in 1493 during Meiō 2. Records of its construction are preserved in inscriptions on ridge beams. The porch underwent repairs in the third year of {{ill|Jōkyō|ja|貞享|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōkyō|qq=}} (1686). It features a unique regional style, with the inner sanctuary raised on a higher platform. Reused old materials under the floor suggest the structure was originally a four-bay building. Designated on June 9, 1960. === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Nationally Designated) === * Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine Shinji Dance *: Designated on May 17, 1977. For details, see ''[[Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine Shinji Dance]]''. === Hyōgo Prefectural Cultural Property === * Shinji Noh Masks *: Twelve noh masks used for the sacred dance survive. Of these, five masks—black old man, young man, father, old man, and king—are still used in ceremonies today. The remaining seven masks (old man, young man, old woman, prince, etc.) have unclear uses. Designated on March 29, 1968. == Gallery == File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 02.JPG|Approach staircase File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 08.JPG|Dance stage (right) and long hall (left) File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 09.JPG|Dance stage == References == * Editorial Committee on Hyōgo Prefecture Historical Walks / ed., ''Historical Walks of Hyōgo Prefecture, Vol. 2,'' {{ill|Yamakawa Shuppansha|ja|山川出版社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamakawa Shuppansha|qq=}} (2006), pp. 61–62. * On-site explanation board. == Related Articles == {{commonscat|Sumiyoshi-jinja (Kamikamogawa, Kato)}} * {{ill|List of Hyōgo Prefectural Cultural Properties|ja|兵庫県指定文化財一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Hyōgo Prefectural Cultural Properties|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://www.kato-kanko.jp/2017/03/sumiyoshi-shrine/ Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine] (Kato City Tourism Association) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|住吉神社 (加東市上鴨川)|version=80255673|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kato, Hyōgo]] [[Category:History of Kato, Hyōgo]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Harima Province]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi Shrines]] [[Category:Muromachi-period architecture]] [[Category:Kami-Kamikawa Sumiyoshi Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Pages that were shrank]] [[Category:Pages likely having an accidental overwite in their history that shrank them]] }} [[ja:住吉神社 (加東市上鴨川)]] [[sn:Kami-Kamikawa Sumiyoshi Shrine]] == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Kami-Kamikawa Sumiyoshi Shrine (Q11381860)''' is a shrine in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. It is located in [[Harima Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | RONSHA || {{ill|Kami-Kamikawa Sumiyoshi Shrine|lt=Sumiyoshi Shrine|ja|住吉神社 (加東市上鴨川)|commons|Category:Sumiyoshi-jinja (Kamikamogawa, Kato)|WD=Q11381860}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized}}[[Category:Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)]] || — || — || {{ill|Sumiyoshi Shrine|lt=Sumiyoshi Shrine|WD=Q135041176}}; {{ill|Sumiyoshi Shrine|lt=Sumiyoshi Shrine|WD=Q135041176}} || {{coord|34.966752|135.059517|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=183049 DB] |} [[Category:Shikinaisha in Harima Province]] [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] {{wikidata link|Q11381860}}

{{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|WD=Q1114013}} | consecration_year=Unknown | Shrine Rank=Shikinaisha (small shrine), Village shrine | Annual festival=Shinji Dance (First Saturday and Sunday of October) | Main hall=[[Sanctuary with nagare-zukuri style]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 03.JPG | Map=Japan Hyōgo | location = {{ill|Kato|WD=Q877164}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.96675|N|135.059556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Kato City]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. The enshrined deities are the [[Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi Sanjin]] (Kamutsutsuno-o no Mikoto, Nakatsutsuno-o no Mikoto, Sokotsutsuno-o no Mikoto) and [[:en:Empress Jingū|Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha (small shrine)|ja|式内小社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha (small shrine)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11064932}} under the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]], and its former shrine Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was that of a village shrine. It is also referred to as the '''Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine''' due to its location. == Overview == The founding of the shrine is unknown. The shrine precinct features a main hall, a divided worship hall (割拝殿), a long hall (長床), and a dance stage (舞殿), arranged around a central courtyard, preserving an ancient shrine layout. It served as the tutelary shrine of Kamikamogawa Village. The main hall is designated as an [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property (Japan)]] of Japan and is recorded to have been constructed in the mid-[[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] during the second year of [[:en:Meiō|Meiō]] (1493). == Cultural Properties == [[File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 07.JPG|thumb|Main hall (National Important Cultural Property)]] === Important Cultural Property (Nationally Designated) === * Main Hall *: The main hall, constructed in the [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] style with a cypress bark roof, was first built in the late [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]] in the fifth year of [[:en:Shōwa (1312–1317)|Shōwa]] (1316). It was rebuilt during the mid-Muromachi period in the sixth year of [[:en:Eikyō|Eikyō]] (1434), and again in 1493 during Meiō 2. Records of its construction are preserved in inscriptions on ridge beams. The porch underwent repairs in the third year of [[:en:Jōkyō|Jōkyō]] (1686). It features a unique regional style, with the inner sanctuary raised on a higher platform. Reused old materials under the floor suggest the structure was originally a four-bay building. Designated on June 9, 1960. === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Nationally Designated) === * Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine Shinji Dance *: Designated on May 17, 1977. For details, see ''[[Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine Shinji Dance]]''. === Hyōgo Prefectural Cultural Property === * Shinji Noh Masks *: Twelve noh masks used for the sacred dance survive. Of these, five masks—black old man, young man, father, old man, and king—are still used in ceremonies today. The remaining seven masks (old man, young man, old woman, prince, etc.) have unclear uses. Designated on March 29, 1968. == Gallery == File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 02.JPG|Approach staircase File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 08.JPG|Dance stage (right) and long hall (left) File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 09.JPG|Dance stage == References == * Editorial Committee on Hyōgo Prefecture Historical Walks / ed., ''Historical Walks of Hyōgo Prefecture, Vol. 2,'' {{ill|Yamakawa Shuppansha|ja|山川出版社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamakawa Shuppansha|qq=}} (2006), pp. 61–62. * On-site explanation board. == Related Articles == {{commonscat|Sumiyoshi-jinja (Kamikamogawa, Kato)}} * {{ill|List of Hyōgo Prefectural Cultural Properties|ja|兵庫県指定文化財一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Hyōgo Prefectural Cultural Properties|1=Hyōgo Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392892}} == External Links == * [https://www.kato-kanko.jp/2017/03/sumiyoshi-shrine/ Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine] (Kato City Tourism Association) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|住吉神社 (加東市上鴨川)|version=80255673|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Harima Province]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:住吉神社 (加東市上鴨川)]] [[Category:Son-sha in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Hyogo prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Kato, Hyogo]] [[Category:Municipal History of Katō]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] {{Commons category|Sumiyoshi-jinja (Kamikamogawa, Kato)}} {{wikidata link|Q11381860}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

Designated on March 29, 1968. == Gallery == File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 02.JPG|Approach staircase File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 08.JPG|Dance stage (right) and long hall (left) File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 09.JPG|Dance stage == References == * Editorial Committee on Hyōgo Prefecture Historical Walks / ed., ''Historical Walks of Hyōgo Prefecture, Vol. 2,'' {{ill|Yamakawa Shuppansha|ja|山川出版社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamakawa Shuppansha|qq=}} (2006), pp. 61–62. * On-site explanation board. == Related Articles == {{commonscat|Sumiyoshi-jinja (Kamikamogawa, Kato)}} * {{ill|List of Hyōgo Prefectural Cultural Properties|ja|兵庫県指定文化財一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Hyōgo Prefectural Cultural Properties|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://www.kato-kanko.jp/2017/03/sumiyoshi-shrine/ Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine] (Kato City Tourism Association) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|住吉神社 (加東市上鴨川)|version=80255673|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kato, Hyōgo]] [[Category:History of Kato, Hyōgo]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Harima Province]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi Shrines]] [[Category:Muromachi-period architecture]] [[Category:Kami-Kamikawa Sumiyoshi Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Pages that were shrank]] [[Category:Pages likely having an accidental overwite in their history that shrank them]] }} [[ja:住吉神社 (加東市上鴨川)]] [[sn:Kami-Kamikawa Sumiyoshi Shrine]] == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Kami-Kamikawa Sumiyoshi Shrine (Q11381860)''' is a shrine in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. It is located in [[Harima Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | RONSHA || {{ill|Kami-Kamikawa Sumiyoshi Shrine|lt=Sumiyoshi Shrine|ja|住吉神社 (加東市上鴨川)|commons|Category:Sumiyoshi-jinja (Kamikamogawa, Kato)|WD=Q11381860}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized}}[[Category:Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)]] || — || — || {{ill|Sumiyoshi Shrine|lt=Sumiyoshi Shrine|WD=Q135041176}}; {{ill|Sumiyoshi Shrine|lt=Sumiyoshi Shrine|WD=Q135041176}} || {{coord|34.966752|135.059517|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=183049 DB] |} [[Category:Shikinaisha in Harima Province]] [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] {{wikidata link|Q11381860}} == Accidentally Overwritten Content == [[Category:Pages with merged translations]] [[sn:commons:Category:Sumiyoshi-jinja (Kamikamogawa, Kato)]][[ja:住吉神社 (加東市上鴨川)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11381860]] Wikidata name: Sumiyoshi-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11381860]] Wikidata name: Sumiyoshi-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Sumiyoshi Shrine |location=[[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[Kato City]], Kamikamo River 571 | Latitude degrees = 34 | Latitude minutes = 58 | Latitude seconds = 0.3 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 135 | Longitude minutes = 3 | Longitude seconds = 34.4 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | deity={{ill|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803}}; {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|WD=Q1114013}} | consecration_year=Unknown | Shrine Rank=Shikinaisha (small shrine), Village shrine | Annual festival=Shinji Dance (First Saturday and Sunday of October) | Main hall=[[Sanctuary with nagare-zukuri style]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 03.JPG | Map=Japan Hyōgo | location = {{ill|Kato|WD=Q877164}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.96675|N|135.059556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Kato City]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. The enshrined deities are the [[Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi Sanjin]] (Kamutsutsuno-o no Mikoto, Nakatsutsuno-o no Mikoto, Sokotsutsuno-o no Mikoto) and [[:en:Empress Jingū|Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha (small shrine)|ja|式内小社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha (small shrine)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11064932}} under the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]], and its former shrine Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was that of a village shrine. It is also referred to as the '''Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine''' due to its location. == Overview == The founding of the shrine is unknown. The shrine precinct features a main hall, a divided worship hall (割拝殿), a long hall (長床), and a dance stage (舞殿), arranged around a central courtyard, preserving an ancient shrine layout. It served as the tutelary shrine of Kamikamogawa Village. The main hall is designated as an [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property (Japan)]] of Japan and is recorded to have been constructed in the mid-[[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] during the second year of [[:en:Meiō|Meiō]] (1493). == Cultural Properties == [[File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 07.JPG|thumb|Main hall (National Important Cultural Property)]] === Important Cultural Property (Nationally Designated) === * Main Hall *: The main hall, constructed in the [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] style with a cypress bark roof, was first built in the late [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]] in the fifth year of [[:en:Shōwa (1312–1317)|Shōwa]] (1316). It was rebuilt during the mid-Muromachi period in the sixth year of [[:en:Eikyō|Eikyō]] (1434), and again in 1493 during Meiō 2. Records of its construction are preserved in inscriptions on ridge beams. The porch underwent repairs in the third year of [[:en:Jōkyō|Jōkyō]] (1686). It features a unique regional style, with the inner sanctuary raised on a higher platform. Reused old materials under the floor suggest the structure was originally a four-bay building. Designated on June 9, 1960. === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Nationally Designated) === * Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine Shinji Dance *: Designated on May 17, 1977. For details, see ''[[Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine Shinji Dance]]''. === Hyōgo Prefectural Cultural Property === * Shinji Noh Masks *: Twelve noh masks used for the sacred dance survive. Of these, five masks—black old man, young man, father, old man, and king—are still used in ceremonies today. The remaining seven masks (old man, young man, old woman, prince, etc.) have unclear uses. Designated on March 29, 1968. == Gallery == File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 02.JPG|Approach staircase File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 08.JPG|Dance stage (right) and long hall (left) File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 09.JPG|Dance stage == References == * Editorial Committee on Hyōgo Prefecture Historical Walks / ed., ''Historical Walks of Hyōgo Prefecture, Vol. 2,'' {{ill|Yamakawa Shuppansha|ja|山川出版社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamakawa Shuppansha|qq=}} (2006), pp. 61–62. * On-site explanation board. == Related Articles == {{commonscat|Sumiyoshi-jinja (Kamikamogawa, Kato)}} * {{ill|List of Hyōgo Prefectural Cultural Properties|ja|兵庫県指定文化財一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Hyōgo Prefectural Cultural Properties|1=Hyōgo Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392892}} == External Links == * [https://www.kato-kanko.jp/2017/03/sumiyoshi-shrine/ Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine] (Kato City Tourism Association) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|住吉神社 (加東市上鴨川)|version=80255673|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Harima Province]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:住吉神社 (加東市上鴨川)]] [[Category:Son-sha in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Hyogo prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Kato, Hyogo]] [[Category:Municipal History of Katō]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] {{Commons category|Sumiyoshi-jinja (Kamikamogawa, Kato)}} {{wikidata link|Q11381860}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

The main hall is designated as an [[Important Cultural Property]] of Japan and is recorded to have been constructed in the mid-[[Muromachi period]] during the second year of {{ill|Meiō|ja|明応|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiō|qq=}} (1493). == Cultural Properties == [[File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 07.JPG|thumb|Main hall (National Important Cultural Property)]] === Important Cultural Property (Nationally Designated) === * Main Hall *: The main hall, constructed in the {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagare-zukuri|qq=}} style with a cypress bark roof, was first built in the late {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura period|qq=}} in the fifth year of {{ill|Shōwa|ja|正和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa|qq=}} (1316). It was rebuilt during the mid-Muromachi period in the sixth year of {{ill|Eikyō|ja|永享|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eikyō|qq=}} (1434), and again in 1493 during Meiō 2. Records of its construction are preserved in inscriptions on ridge beams. The porch underwent repairs in the third year of {{ill|Jōkyō|ja|貞享|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōkyō|qq=}} (1686). It features a unique regional style, with the inner sanctuary raised on a higher platform. Reused old materials under the floor suggest the structure was originally a four-bay building. Designated on June 9, 1960. === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Nationally Designated) === * Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine Shinji Dance *: Designated on May 17, 1977. For details, see ''[[Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine Shinji Dance]]''. === Hyōgo Prefectural Cultural Property === * Shinji Noh Masks *: Twelve noh masks used for the sacred dance survive. Of these, five masks—black old man, young man, father, old man, and king—are still used in ceremonies today. The remaining seven masks (old man, young man, old woman, prince, etc.) have unclear uses. Designated on March 29, 1968. == Gallery == File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 02.JPG|Approach staircase File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 08.JPG|Dance stage (right) and long hall (left) File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 09.JPG|Dance stage == References == * Editorial Committee on Hyōgo Prefecture Historical Walks / ed., ''Historical Walks of Hyōgo Prefecture, Vol. 2,'' {{ill|Yamakawa Shuppansha|ja|山川出版社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamakawa Shuppansha|qq=}} (2006), pp. 61–62. * On-site explanation board. == Related Articles == {{commonscat|Sumiyoshi-jinja (Kamikamogawa, Kato)}} * {{ill|List of Hyōgo Prefectural Cultural Properties|ja|兵庫県指定文化財一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Hyōgo Prefectural Cultural Properties|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://www.kato-kanko.jp/2017/03/sumiyoshi-shrine/ Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine] (Kato City Tourism Association) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|住吉神社 (加東市上鴨川)|version=80255673|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kato, Hyōgo]] [[Category:History of Kato, Hyōgo]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Harima Province]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi Shrines]] [[Category:Muromachi-period architecture]] [[Category:Kami-Kamikawa Sumiyoshi Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Pages that were shrank]] [[Category:Pages likely having an accidental overwite in their history that shrank them]] }} [[ja:住吉神社 (加東市上鴨川)]] [[sn:Kami-Kamikawa Sumiyoshi Shrine]] == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Kami-Kamikawa Sumiyoshi Shrine (Q11381860)''' is a shrine in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. The enshrined deities are the [[Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi Sanjin]] (Kamutsutsuno-o no Mikoto, Nakatsutsuno-o no Mikoto, Sokotsutsuno-o no Mikoto) and [[:en:Empress Jingū|Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha (small shrine)|ja|式内小社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha (small shrine)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11064932}} under the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]], and its former shrine Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was that of a village shrine. It is also referred to as the '''Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine''' due to its location. == Overview == The founding of the shrine is unknown. The shrine precinct features a main hall, a divided worship hall (割拝殿), a long hall (長床), and a dance stage (舞殿), arranged around a central courtyard, preserving an ancient shrine layout. It served as the tutelary shrine of Kamikamogawa Village. The main hall is designated as an [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property (Japan)]] of Japan and is recorded to have been constructed in the mid-[[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] during the second year of [[:en:Meiō|Meiō]] (1493). == Cultural Properties == [[File:Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Jinja 07.JPG|thumb|Main hall (National Important Cultural Property)]] === Important Cultural Property (Nationally Designated) === * Main Hall *: The main hall, constructed in the [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] style with a cypress bark roof, was first built in the late [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]] in the fifth year of [[:en:Shōwa (1312–1317)|Shōwa]] (1316). It was rebuilt during the mid-Muromachi period in the sixth year of [[:en:Eikyō|Eikyō]] (1434), and again in 1493 during Meiō 2. Records of its construction are preserved in inscriptions on ridge beams. The porch underwent repairs in the third year of [[:en:Jōkyō|Jōkyō]] (1686). It features a unique regional style, with the inner sanctuary raised on a higher platform. Reused old materials under the floor suggest the structure was originally a four-bay building. Designated on June 9, 1960. === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Nationally Designated) === * Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine Shinji Dance *: Designated on May 17, 1977. For details, see ''[[Kamikamogawa Sumiyoshi Shrine Shinji Dance]]''. === Hyōgo Prefectural Cultural Property === * Shinji Noh Masks *: Twelve noh masks used for the sacred dance survive. Of these, five masks—black old man, young man, father, old man, and king—are still used in ceremonies today. The remaining seven masks (old man, young man, old woman, prince, etc.) have unclear uses.
